1→#' Construct a `projector` instance
     2→#'
     3→#' A `projector` maps a matrix from an N-dimensional space to d-dimensional space, where `d` may be less than `N`.
     4→#' The projection matrix, `v`, is not necessarily orthogonal. This function constructs a `projector` instance which can be
     5→#' used for various dimensionality reduction techniques like PCA, LDA, etc.
     6→#'
     7→#' @param v A matrix of coefficients with dimensions `nrow(v)` by `ncol(v)` (columns = components)
     8→#' @param preproc A prepped pre-processing object (S3 class `pre_processor`). Default is the no-op `pass()` preprocessor.
     9→#' @param classes Additional class information used for creating subtypes of `projector`. Default is NULL.
    10→#' @param ... Extra arguments to be stored in the `projector` object.
    11→#'
    12→#' @return An instance of type `projector`.
    13→#'
    14→#' @export
    15→projector <- function(v, preproc = prep(pass()), ..., classes = NULL) {
    16→  # B3: Ensure v is matrix
    17→  if (!is.matrix(v)) {
    18→    v <- as.matrix(v)
    19→  }
    20→  
    21→  # chkor_vld(chk::chk_matrix(v), chk::chk_s4_class(v, "Matrix")) # Removed: Redundant after as.matrix and caused error
    22→  chk::chk_s3_class(preproc, "pre_processor")
    23→  
    24→  out <- structure(
    25→    list(
    26→      v       = v,
    27→      preproc = preproc,
    28→      ...), 
    29→    class = c(classes, "projector")
    30→  )
    31→  
    32→  # Add cache environment attribute
    33→  attr(out, ".cache") <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())
    34→  out
    35→}
    36→
    37→#' @export
    38→components.projector <- function(x, ...) {
    39→  x$v
    40→}
    41→
    42→
    43→
    44→#' @export
    45→coef.projector <- function(object, ...) {
    46→  components(object)
    47→}
    48→
    49→
    50→#' @export
    51→#' @importFrom assertthat assert_that
    52→ncomp.projector <- function(x) {
    53→  assertthat::assert_that(inherits(x, "projector"))
    54→  ncol(components(x))
    55→}
    56→
    57→#' Stricter check for true orthogonality
    58→#'
    59→#' We test if v^T * v = I (when rows >= cols) or v * v^T = I (when cols > rows).
    60→#'
    61→#' @param tol tolerance for checking orthogonality
    62→#' @param x the projector object
    63→#' @export
    64→#' @importFrom assertthat assert_that
    65→is_orthogonal.projector <- function(x, tol=1e-6) {
    66→  assertthat::assert_that(inherits(x, "projector"))
    67→  v <- components(x)
    68→  
    69→  # If p >= d, check crossprod(v). Otherwise, check tcrossprod(v).
    70→  if (nrow(v) >= ncol(v)) {
    71→    mat <- crossprod(v)  # d x d
    72→  } else {
    73→    mat <- tcrossprod(v) # p x p
    74→  }
    75→  
    76→  # We want it ~ identity. Check diagonal ~ 1 and off-diagonal ~ 0
    77→  diag_elems <- diag(mat)
    78→  off_diag   <- mat - diag(diag_elems)
    79→  all(abs(diag_elems - 1) < tol) && all(abs(off_diag) < tol)
    80→}
    81→
    82→#' Possibly use ridge-regularized inversion of crossprod(v)
    83→#' @keywords internal
    84→robust_inv_vTv <- function(v, lambda = 1e-6) {
    85→  vt_v   <- crossprod(v)                # d x d
    86→  vt_v_r <- vt_v + diag(lambda, nrow(vt_v))
    87→  solve(vt_v_r)
    88→}
    89→
    90→
    91→#' @importFrom assertthat assert_that
    92→inverse_projection.projector <- function(x, ...) {
    93→  assertthat::assert_that(inherits(x, "projector"))
    94→  
    95→  # Robust caching check
    96→  cache_env <- attr(x, ".cache")
    97→  use_caching <- !is.null(cache_env) && is.environment(cache_env)
    98→  key <- "inv_proj"
    99→
   100→  if (use_caching && !is.null(cache_env[[key]])) {
   101→    return(cache_env[[key]])
   102→  }
   103→
   104→  # Compute if not cached or cache not available
   105→  v <- coef(x)
   106→  if (!requireNamespace("corpcor", quiet=TRUE)) {
   107→    stop("package corpcor required for inverse_projection.")
   108→  }
   109→  inv_p <- corpcor::pseudoinverse(v)
   110→
   111→  # Store in cache if available
   112→  if (use_caching) {
   113→    cache_env[[key]] <- inv_p
   114→  }
   115→
   116→  inv_p
   117→}
   118→
   119→
   120→#' @export
   121→partial_inverse_projection.projector <- function(x, colind, ...) {
   122→  assertthat::assert_that(inherits(x, "projector"))
   123→  chk::chk_vector(colind)
   124→  chk::chk_whole_numeric(colind)
   125→  chk::chk_range(max(colind, na.rm=TRUE), c(1, ncol(coef(x))))
   126→  chk::chk_range(min(colind, na.rm=TRUE), c(1, ncol(coef(x))))
   127→
   128→  # Robust caching check
   129→  cache_env <- attr(x, ".cache")
   130→  use_caching <- !is.null(cache_env) && is.environment(cache_env)
   131→  key <- paste0(".pinv_v_subset_", paste(sort(unique(colind)), collapse = "_"))
   132→
   133→  if (use_caching && !is.null(cache_env[[key]])) {
   134→    return(cache_env[[key]])
   135→  }
   136→
   137→  # Compute if not cached or cache not available
   138→  v <- coef(x)[, colind, drop=FALSE]
   139→  if (!requireNamespace("corpcor", quiet=TRUE)) {
   140→      stop("package corpcor required for partial_inverse_projection.")
   141→  }
   142→  pinv_sub <- corpcor::pseudoinverse(v)
   143→
   144→  # Store in cache if available
   145→  if (use_caching) {
   146→    cache_env[[key]] <- pinv_sub
   147→  }
   148→
   149→  pinv_sub
   150→}
   151→
   152→
   153→#' @export
   154→truncate.projector <- function(x, ncomp) {
   155→  old_ncomp <- ncomp(x)
   156→  chk::chk_number(ncomp)
   157→  if (ncomp < 1 || ncomp > old_ncomp) {
   158→    stop("Requested ncomp must be between 1 and ", old_ncomp)
   159→  }
   160→  
   161→  v_new       <- components(x)[, seq_len(ncomp), drop = FALSE]
   162→  x$v         <- v_new
   163→  cache_env   <- attr(x, ".cache")
   164→  if (!is.null(cache_env)) {
   165→    rm(list = ls(cache_env), envir = cache_env)  # Clear the cache
   166→  }
   167→  x
   168→}
   169→
   170→
   171→#' @export
   172→reprocess.projector <- function(x, new_data, colind = NULL, ...) {
   173→  p <- nrow(components(x))
   174→  if (is.null(colind)) {
   175→    # Full dimension
   176→    chk::chk_equal(ncol(new_data), p)
   177→    apply_transform(x$preproc, new_data)
   178→  } else {
   179→    chk::chk_equal(length(colind), ncol(new_data))
   180→    apply_transform(x$preproc, new_data, colind)
   181→  }
   182→}
   183→
   184→#' @export
   185→shape.projector <- function(x, ...) {
   186→  dim(components(x))
   187→}
   188→
   189→
   190→#' @export
   191→project.projector <- function(x, new_data, ...) {
   192→  if (is.vector(new_data)) {
   193→    chk::chk_equal(length(new_data), shape(x)[1])
   194→    new_data <- matrix(new_data, nrow = 1)
   195→  }
   196→  chk::vld_matrix(new_data)
   197→  chk::chk_equal(ncol(new_data), nrow(components(x)))
   198→  
   199→  # Reprocess
   200→  nd_proc <- reprocess(x, new_data)
   201→  nd_proc %*% components(x)
   202→}
   203→
   204→
   205→#' @export
   206→partial_project.projector <- function(x,
   207→                                      new_data,
   208→                                      colind,
   209→                                      least_squares = TRUE,
   210→                                      lambda = 1e-6,
   211→                                      ...)
   212→{
   213→  # shape checks
   214→  if (is.vector(new_data) && length(colind) > 1) {
   215→    new_data <- matrix(new_data, nrow = 1)
   216→  } else if (is.vector(new_data) && length(colind) == 1) {
   217→    new_data <- matrix(new_data, ncol = 1)
   218→  }
   219→  chk::chk_equal(ncol(new_data), length(colind))
   220→  
   221→  # reprocess partial
   222→  nd_proc <- reprocess(x, new_data, colind)
   223→  
   224→  v_sub <- components(x)[colind, , drop = FALSE]  # subset
   225→  if (least_squares) {
   226→    inv_vt_v   <- robust_inv_vTv(v_sub, lambda = lambda)  # (d x d)
   227→    factor_scr <- nd_proc %*% v_sub %*% inv_vt_v
   228→  } else {
   229→    factor_scr <- nd_proc %*% v_sub
   230→  }
   231→  factor_scr
   232→}
   233→
   234→
   235→#' @export
   236→reconstruct_new.projector <- function(x,
   237→                                      new_data,
   238→                                      colind         = NULL,
   239→                                      least_squares  = TRUE,
   240→                                      lambda         = 1e-6,
   241→                                      ...)
   242→{
   243→  v_full <- components(x)
   244→  
   245→  if (is.null(colind)) {
   246→    # Reconstruct all columns => new_data is (n x p)
   247→    nd_proc <- reprocess(x, new_data, colind = NULL)
   248→    if (least_squares) {
   249→      inv_vt_v    <- robust_inv_vTv(v_full, lambda = lambda)
   250→      factor_scr  <- nd_proc %*% v_full %*% inv_vt_v
   251→    } else {
   252→      factor_scr  <- nd_proc %*% v_full
   253→    }
   254→    rec_data <- factor_scr %*% t(v_full)
   255→    
   256→    # If you wish, do a reverse transform on rec_data:
   257→    # Apply reverse transform to return to original space
   258→    reverse_transform(x$preproc, rec_data)
   259→  } else {
   260→    # PARTIAL reconstruction => new_data is (n x length(colind))
   261→    factor_scr <- partial_project.projector(
   262→      x, new_data, colind,
   263→      least_squares = least_squares,
   264→      lambda        = lambda
   265→    )
   266→    v_sub    <- v_full[colind, , drop = FALSE]
   267→    rec_data <- factor_scr %*% t(v_sub)
   268→    
   269→    # Similarly, you could do a partial reverse_transform:
   270→    # Apply reverse transform (potentially partial if needed) to return to original space
   271→    # Assuming reverse_transform handles partial cases correctly based on input dim
   272→    reverse_transform(x$preproc, rec_data)
   273→  }
   274→}
   275→
   276→
   277→#' @export
   278→print.projector <- function(x, ...) {
   279→  cat(crayon::bold(crayon::green("Projector object:\n")))
   280→  cat(crayon::yellow("  Input dimension: "),  shape(x)[1], "\n", sep = "")
   281→  cat(crayon::yellow("  Output dimension: "), shape(x)[2], "\n", sep = "")
   282→  
   283→  if (!is.null(x$preproc)) {
   284→    cat(crayon::cyan("  With pre-processing:\n"))
   285→    if (inherits(x$preproc, "pre_processor")) {
   286→      print(x$preproc)
   287→    } else {
   288→      cat(crayon::cyan("    (pre-processing pipeline not fully available)\n"))
   289→    }
   290→  } else {
   291→    cat(crayon::cyan("  No pre-processing pipeline.\n"))
   292→  }
   293→  invisible(x)
   294→}
   295→
   296→
   297→
   298→#' @export
   299→partial_projector.projector <- function(x, colind, ...) {
   300→  # We'll store a reference to the original (full) projector in x$porig
   301→  # so partial calls can delegate back.
   302→  new_obj <- projector(x$v[colind, ],
   303→                       preproc = x$preproc,
   304→                       colind  = colind,
   305→                       porig   = x,
   306→                       classes = "partial_projector")
   307→  new_obj
   308→}
   309→
   310→
   311→#' @export
   312→reprocess.partial_projector <- function(x, new_data, colind = NULL, ...) {
   313→  chk::chk_not_null(x$colind)
   314→  
   315→  if (is.null(colind)) {
   316→    # Full dimension = length(x$colind)
   317→    chk::chk_equal(ncol(new_data), nrow(components(x)))
   318→    apply_transform(x$preproc, new_data)
   319→  } else {
   320→    chk::chk_equal(length(colind), ncol(new_data))
   321→    base_colind <- x$colind
   322→    chk::chk_not_null(base_colind)
   323→    # Now map the local colind to the original projector's colind
   324→    # e.g. base_colind[colind]
   325→    apply_transform(x$preproc, new_data, base_colind[colind])
   326→  }
   327→}
   328→
   329→
   330→#' @export
   331→project.partial_projector <- function(x, new_data, ...) {
   332→  chk::chk_not_null(x$porig)
   333→  chk::chk_not_null(x$colind)
   334→  partial_project(x$porig, new_data, x$colind, ...)
   335→}
   336→
   337→
   338→#' @export
   339→truncate.partial_projector <- function(x, ncomp) {
   340→  chk::chk_not_null(x$porig)
   341→  old_ncomp <- ncomp(x)
   342→  if (ncomp < 1 || ncomp > old_ncomp) {
   343→    stop("Requested ncomp must be between 1 and ", old_ncomp)
   344→  }
   345→  
   346→  porig_trunc <- truncate(x$porig, ncomp)  # preserve extra fields
   347→  # partial_projector from the truncated original
   348→  partial_projector(porig_trunc, x$colind)
   349→}
   350→
   351→#' @export
   352→partial_project.partial_projector <- function(x, new_data, colind, ...) {
   353→  # Ensure we have a reference to the original projector and the stored colind
   354→  if (is.null(x$porig) || is.null(x$colind)) {
   355→    stop("`x` must have `porig` (original projector) and `colind` fields.")
   356→  }
   357→  
   358→  # Local check: colind must be within [1, length(x$colind)]
   359→  n_local_cols <- length(x$colind)
   360→  if (any(colind < 1) || any(colind > n_local_cols)) {
   361→    stop(
   362→      "Requested columns (", paste(colind, collapse=", "), 
   363→      ") exceed local partial projector's range [1..", n_local_cols, "]."
   364→    )
   365→  }
   366→  
   367→  # Map the local colind to the original projector's colind
   368→  global_colind <- x$colind[colind]
   369→  
   370→  # Delegate to the original projector's partial_project
   371→  partial_project(x$porig, new_data, global_colind, ...)
   372→}

     5→#' variable space to the d-dimensional component space. The singular value decomposition is a canonical example of such a two-way mapping.
     6→#'

     8→#' @param s The score matrix
     9→#' @param sdev The standard deviations of the score matrix
    10→#' @param preproc (optional) A pre-processing pipeline, default is prep(pass())
    11→#' @param classes (optional) A character vector specifying the class attributes of the object, default is NULL

    20→  chk::vld_matrix(v)
    21→  chk::vld_matrix(s)
    22→  chk::vld_numeric(sdev)
    23→  chk::chk_equal(length(sdev), ncol(s))

#' Construct a bi_projector instance
#'
#' A bi_projector offers a two-way mapping from samples (rows) to scores and from variables (columns) to components.
#' Thus, one can project from D-dimensional input space to d-dimensional subspace. And one can project (project_vars) from n-dimensional
#' variable space to the d-dimensional component space. The singular value decomposition is a canonical example of such a two-way mapping.
#'
#' @inheritParams projector
#' @param s The score matrix
#' @param sdev The standard deviations of the score matrix
#' @param preproc (optional) A pre-processing pipeline, default is prep(pass())
#' @param classes (optional) A character vector specifying the class attributes of the object, default is NULL
#' @return A bi_projector object
#' @examples
#' X <- matrix(rnorm(200), 10, 20)
#' svdfit <- svd(X)
#'
#' p <- bi_projector(svdfit$v, s = svdfit$u %*% diag(svdfit$d), sdev=svdfit$d)
#' @export
bi_projector <- function(v, s, sdev, preproc=prep(pass()), classes=NULL, ...) {
  chk::vld_matrix(v)
  chk::vld_matrix(s)
  chk::vld_numeric(sdev)
  chk::chk_equal(length(sdev), ncol(s))
  chk::chk_equal(ncol(v), length(sdev))
  
  out <- projector(v, preproc=preproc, s=s, sdev=sdev, classes=c(classes, "bi_projector"), ...)
}



#' @export
scores.bi_projector <- function(x,...) {
  x$s
}

#' @export
sdev.bi_projector <- function(x) {
  x$sdev
}

#' @export
project_vars.bi_projector <- function(x, new_data, ...) {
  if (is.vector(new_data)) {
    new_data <- matrix(new_data, ncol = 1)
  }
  chk::chk_matrix(new_data)
  
  sc <- scores(x)
  chk::chk_equal(nrow(new_data), nrow(sc))
  
  variance <- sdev(x)^2
  # Check for zero or near-zero variance  
  if (any(abs(variance) < 1e-12)) {
    warning("Some variance values are near zero; results may be unstable.")
  }
  
  t(new_data) %*% sc %*% diag(1/variance, nrow=length(variance), ncol=length(variance))
}

#' @keywords internal
#' @noRd
genreconstruct <- function(x, comp, rowind, colind) {
  ip <- inverse_projection(x)
  out <- scores(x)[rowind,comp,drop=FALSE] %*% ip[comp,,drop=FALSE][,colind,drop=FALSE]
  reverse_transform(x$preproc, out, colind)
}

#' @export
reconstruct.bi_projector <- function(x, comp=1:ncomp(x), rowind=1:nrow(scores(x)), 
                                     colind=1:nrow(coefficients(x)), ...) {
  chk::chk_numeric(comp)
  chk::chk_true(max(comp) <= ncomp(x))
  chk::chk_numeric(rowind)
  chk::chk_numeric(colind)
  chk::chk_range(comp, c(1,ncomp(x)))
  chk::chk_range(rowind, c(1,nrow(scores(x))))
  chk::chk_range(colind, c(1,nrow(coef.projector(x))))
  genreconstruct(x,comp, rowind, colind)
}

#' @export
residuals.bi_projector <- function(x, ncomp=ncomp(x), xorig,...) {
  recon <- reconstruct(x, comp=1:ncomp)
  xorig - recon
}



#' Pretty Print S3 Method for bi_projector Class
#'
#' @param x A `bi_projector` object
#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to the print function
#' @return Invisible `bi_projector` object
#' @export
print.bi_projector <- function(x, ...) {
  cat("A bi_projector object with the following properties:\n\n")
  
  cat("Dimensions of the weights (v) matrix:\n")
  cat("  Rows: ", nrow(x$v), " Columns: ", ncol(x$v), "\n")
  
  cat("\nDimensions of the scores (s) matrix:\n")
  cat("  Rows: ", nrow(x$s), " Columns: ", ncol(x$s), "\n")
  
  cat("\nLength of the standard deviations (sdev) vector:\n")
  cat("  Length: ", length(x$sdev), "\n")
  
  cat("\nPreprocessing information:\n")
  print(x$preproc, ...)
  
  invisible(x)
}

#' @export
truncate.bi_projector <- function(x, ncomp) {
  old_ncomp <- ncomp(x)
  chk::chk_number(ncomp)
  if (ncomp < 1 || ncomp > old_ncomp) {
    stop("Requested ncomp must be between 1 and ", old_ncomp)
  }
  
  # Truncate projector components
  v_new <- components(x)[, seq_len(ncomp), drop = FALSE]
  x$v   <- v_new
  
  # Truncate scores and sdev specific to bi_projector
  if (!is.null(x$s)) {
      x$s <- x$s[, seq_len(ncomp), drop = FALSE]
  }
  if (!is.null(x$sdev)) {
      x$sdev <- x$sdev[seq_len(ncomp)]
  }
  
  # Clear any cached inverse projection etc.
  cache_env   <- attr(x, ".cache")
  if (!is.null(cache_env) && is.environment(cache_env)) {
    rm(list = ls(cache_env), envir = cache_env)
  }
  x
}

#' @export
reconstruct_new.bi_projector <- function(x, 
                                         new_data,
                                         comp = 1:ncomp(x),
                                         colind = 1:nrow(coef.projector(x)),
                                         ...)
{
  # Validate inputs
  chk::chk_subset(comp, 1:ncomp(x))
  chk::chk_subset(colind, 1:nrow(coef.projector(x)))
  
  # Validate new_data
  if (is.vector(new_data)) {
    new_data <- matrix(new_data, nrow = 1)
  }
  chk::chk_matrix(new_data)
  
  # Handle empty selections
  if (length(comp) == 0 || length(colind) == 0) {
    return(matrix(0, nrow = nrow(new_data), ncol = length(colind)))
  }
  
  # Check dimensions after preprocessing
  chk::chk_equal(ncol(new_data), length(colind))
  
  # Use standard project method for consistency
  # Apply preprocessing only to the provided columns
  full_data_proc <- matrix(0, nrow = nrow(new_data),
                           ncol = nrow(coef.projector(x)))
  full_data_proc[, colind] <- apply_transform(x$preproc, new_data, colind)
  
  # Project to get scores for all components
  scores_new_full <- project(x, full_data_proc)
  
  # Select only the requested components
  scores_new <- scores_new_full[, comp, drop = FALSE]
  
  # Reconstruct using selected components and columns
  # Get the inverse projection for the selected components
  ip <- inverse_projection(x)
  ip_sub <- ip[comp, colind, drop = FALSE]
  
  # Reconstruct
  rec_data_sub <- scores_new %*% ip_sub

  # Reverse transform for the selected columns
  rec_data_sub <- reverse_transform(x$preproc, rec_data_sub, colind)
  rec_data_sub
}

1→#' Principal Components Analysis (PCA)
     2→#'
     3→#' Compute the directions of maximal variance in a data matrix using the Singular Value Decomposition (SVD).
     4→#'
     5→#' @param X The data matrix.
     6→#' @param ncomp The number of requested components to estimate (default is the minimum dimension of the data matrix).
     7→#' @param preproc The pre-processing function to apply to the data matrix (default is centering).
     8→#' @param method The SVD method to use, passed to \code{svd_wrapper} (default is "fast").
     9→#' @param ... Extra arguments to send to \code{svd_wrapper}.
    10→#' @return A \code{bi_projector} object containing the PCA results.
    11→#' @export
    12→#' @seealso \code{\link{svd_wrapper}} for details on SVD methods.
    13→#' @examples
    14→#' data(iris)
    15→#' X <- as.matrix(iris[, 1:4])
    16→#' res <- pca(X, ncomp = 4)
    17→#' tres <- truncate(res, 3)
    18→pca <- function(X, ncomp=min(dim(X)), preproc=center(), 
    19→                method = c("fast", "base", "irlba", "propack", "rsvd", "svds"), ...) {
    20→  chk::chkor_vld(chk::vld_matrix(X), chk::vld_s4_class(X, "Matrix"))
    21→  
    22→  method <- match.arg(method)
    23→  svdres <- svd_wrapper(X, ncomp, preproc, method=method, ...)
    24→  
    25→  ## todo add rownames slot to `bi_projector`?
    26→  if (!is.null(row.names(scores))) {
    27→    row.names(scores) <- row.names(X)[seq_along(svdres$d)]
    28→  }
    29→  
    30→
    31→  attr(svdres, "class") <- c("pca", attr(svdres, "class"))
    32→  svdres
    33→}

    36→#' @keywords internal
    37→#' @noRd
    38→orth_distances.pca <- function(x, ncomp, xorig) {
    39→  resid <- residuals(x, ncomp, xorig)
    40→  scores <- scores(x)
    41→  loadings <- coef(x)
    42→  
    43→  scoresn <- x$u
    44→  
    45→  Q <- matrix(0, nrow = nrow(scores), ncol = ncomp)
    46→  
    47→  for (i in seq_len(ncomp)) {
    48→    res <- resid
    49→    if (i < ncomp) {
    50→      res <- res +
    51→        tcrossprod(
    52→          scores[, (i + 1):ncomp, drop = F],
    53→          loadings[, (i + 1):ncomp, drop = F]
    54→        )
    55→    }
    56→    
    57→    Q[, i] <- rowSums(res^2)
    58→    #T2[, i] <- rowSums(scoresn[, seq_len(i), drop = F]^2)
    59→  }
    60→  
    61→  Q
    62→}

    65→#' @keywords internal
    66→#' @noRd
    67→score_distances.pca <- function(x, ncomp, xorig) {
    68→  scores <- scores(x)
    69→  loadings <- coef(x)
    70→  
    71→  scoresn <- x$u
    72→  
    73→  T2 <- matrix(0, nrow = nrow(scores), ncol = ncomp)
    74→  for (i in seq_len(ncomp)) {
    75→    T2[, i] <- rowSums(scoresn[, seq_len(i), drop = F]^2)
    76→  }
    77→  
    78→  T2
    79→  
    80→}

    83→#' @export
    84→#' @importFrom chk chk_range
    85→truncate.pca <- function(x, ncomp) {
    86→  chk::chk_range(ncomp, c(1, ncomp(x)))
    87→  x$v <- x$v[,1:ncomp, drop=FALSE]
    88→  x$sdev <- x$sdev[1:ncomp]
    89→  x$s <- x$s[,1:ncomp,drop=FALSE]
    90→  x$u <- x$u[, 1:ncomp, drop=FALSE]
    91→  x
    92→}

    97→#' @noRd
    98→perm_ci.pca <- function(x, X, nperm=100, k=4, distr="gamma", parallel=FALSE, ...) {
    99→  .Deprecated("perm_test.pca", package="multivarious", 
   100→              msg = "'perm_ci.pca' is deprecated. Please use 'perm_test.pca' with distribution=\"empirical\" instead.")
   101→  
   102→  # Minimal forwarding logic, likely won't match exactly but guides user
   103→  perm_test.pca(x = x, X = X, nperm = nperm, comps = k, 
   104→                distribution = "empirical", # Force empirical as distr is gone
   105→                parallel = parallel, alternative = "greater", ...)
   106→}

   108→
   109→#' @importFrom stats quantile na.omit
   110→#' @importFrom future.apply future_lapply
   111→#' @export
   112→#' @seealso \code{\link{perm_test}}, \code{\link{pca}}
   113→perm_test.pca <- function(x,
   114→                          X,
   115→                          nperm = 1000,
   116→                          measure_fun = NULL,
   117→                          fit_fun = NULL, # Ignored
   118→                          shuffle_fun = NULL,               
   119→                          stepwise = TRUE,
   120→                          parallel = FALSE,
   121→                          alternative = c("greater", "less", "two.sided"),
   122→                          alpha = 0.05, # Significance level for stopping rule
   123→                          comps = 4,
   124→                          use_svd_solver = c("fast", "RSpectra"), 
   125→                          ...) 
   126→{
   127→  # Match args
   128→  alternative <- match.arg(alternative)
   129→  use_svd_solver <- match.arg(use_svd_solver)
   130→  chk::chk_number(alpha)
   131→  chk::chk_range(alpha, c(0, 1))
   132→  
   133→  # Capture extra args (though likely unused by defaults here)
   134→  extra_args <- list(...)
   135→  
   136→  # --- Setup ---
   137→  Q <- ncomp(x)
   138→  orig_comps <- comps
   139→  comps <- min(Q - 1, comps) # Can only test up to Q-1 components sequentially
   140→  if (comps < 1) stop("Cannot perform permutation test, need at least 2 components in the model.")
   141→  if (comps < orig_comps) {
   142→      message(sprintf("Requested comps=%d truncated to %d (can test at most Q-1=%d components sequentially).", 
   143→                      orig_comps, comps, Q - 1))
   144→  }
   145→  
   146→  # --- Observed Statistics (Default: F_a) ---
   147→  evals <- x$sdev^2
   148→  if (is.null(measure_fun)) {
   149→      observed_stats <- sapply(1:comps, function(a) {
   150→          denom <- sum(evals[a:Q])
   151→          if (denom <= .Machine$double.eps) return(0)
   152→          evals[a] / denom
   153→      })
   154→      stat_name <- "F_a (Fraction of Remaining Variance)"
   155→      internal_stat_fun <- function(modp, a, ...) { # Add ... to signature for consistency
   156→          if (is.null(modp) || length(modp$sdev) == 0) return(NA_real_)
   157→          eperm <- modp$sdev^2
   158→          if (length(eperm) == 0) return(NA_real_)
   159→          n_relevant_evals <- Q - (a - 1)
   160→          if (length(eperm) < 1) return(NA_real_)
   161→          if (length(eperm) < n_relevant_evals) {
   162→              warning(sprintf("Permutation for component %d yielded only %d eigenvalues (expected at least %d). Using sum of available.", a, length(eperm), n_relevant_evals))
   163→              n_relevant_evals <- length(eperm)
   164→          }
   165→          denom <- sum(eperm[1:n_relevant_evals])
   166→          if (denom <= .Machine$double.eps) return(0)
   167→          eperm[1] / denom
   168→      }
   169→  } else {
   170→      stat_name <- deparse(substitute(measure_fun))
   171→      # Calculate observed stats using user function (pass extra args)
   172→      observed_stats <- sapply(1:comps, function(a) do.call(measure_fun, c(list(model_perm = x, comp_idx = a), extra_args)))
   173→      # Internal function passes ... down
   174→      internal_stat_fun <- function(modp, a, ...) {
   175→          if (is.null(modp)) return(NA_real_)
   176→          do.call(measure_fun, c(list(model_perm = modp, comp_idx = a), list(...)))
   177→      }
   178→  }
   179→  
   180→  # --- Default Shuffle Function (Column-wise) ---
   181→  if (is.null(shuffle_fun)) {
   182→      shuffle_fun <- function(dat, ...) apply(dat, 2, sample)
   183→      shuffle_type <- "column-wise"
   184→  } else {
   185→      shuffle_type <- "custom"
   186→  }
   187→  
   188→  # --- Preprocess Data ---
   189→  Xp <- apply_transform(x$preproc, X)
   190→  
   191→  # --- Efficient PCA/SVD for Permutations & P3 Projection ---
   192→  get_leading_svd_u <- function(M, k) {
   193→      if (k <= 0) return(matrix(0.0, nrow = nrow(M), ncol = 0))
   194→      if (nrow(M) < 1 || ncol(M) < 1 || k > min(nrow(M), ncol(M))) {
   195→          warning(sprintf("Cannot compute %d singular vectors for %d x %d matrix.", k, nrow(M), ncol(M)))
   196→          return(NULL)
   197→      }
   198→      solver <- use_svd_solver
   199→      if (solver == "RSpectra" && !requireNamespace("RSpectra", quietly = TRUE)) {
   200→          warning("RSpectra package not found, falling back to use_svd_solver='fast'.")
   201→          solver <- "fast"
   202→      }
   203→      U_perm <- tryCatch({
   204→          if (solver == "RSpectra") RSpectra::svds(M, k = k, nu = k, nv = 0)$u
   205→          else svd(M, nu = k, nv = 0)$u
   206→       }, error = function(e) {
   207→           warning(sprintf("SVD(k=%d) calculation failed: %s. Returning NULL.", k, e$message))
   208→           NULL
   209→       })
   210→      U_perm
   211→  }
   212→  run_pca_perm <- function(M_perm) {
   213→      ncomp_needed <- Q
   214→      pca_res <- try(svd_wrapper(M_perm, ncomp=ncomp_needed, preproc=pass(), method=use_svd_solver), silent=TRUE)
   215→      if (inherits(pca_res, "try-error")) {
   216→          warning(sprintf("PCA failed during permutation: %s", pca_res))
   217→          return(NULL)
   218→      }
   219→      if (is.null(pca_res$sdev) || length(pca_res$sdev) == 0) {
   220→          warning("PCA during permutation resulted in no valid singular values.")
   221→          return(NULL)
   222→      }
   223→      return(pca_res)
   224→  }
   225→  
   226→  # --- Pre-calculate Reconstructions if Stepwise ---
   227→  recon_list <- list()
   228→  if (stepwise && comps > 0) {
   229→      message("Pre-calculating reconstructions for stepwise testing...")
   230→      for (a in 1:comps) {
   231→          cnums <- 1:a
   232→          recon_list[[a]] <- reconstruct(x, comp = cnums)
   233→      }
   234→  }
   235→  I_mat <- diag(nrow(Xp))
   236→  
   237→  # ---------- Permutation Loop Function ----------
   238→  one_perm <- function(perm_idx, current_a, ...) {
   239→      Ea <- if (!stepwise || current_a == 1) Xp else Xp - recon_list[[current_a - 1]]
   240→      
   241→      # Create args for shuffle_fun
   242→      shuffle_args <- c(list(dat = Ea), list(...))
   243→      Ea_perm <- do.call(shuffle_fun, shuffle_args)
   244→      
   245→      Ea_perm_proj <- if (stepwise && current_a > 1) {
   246→          Ua_perm <- get_leading_svd_u(Ea_perm, current_a - 1)
   247→          if (is.null(Ua_perm)) return(NA_real_)
   248→          P_orth <- I_mat - tcrossprod(Ua_perm)
   249→          proj_res <- P_orth %*% Ea_perm
   250→          if (any(!is.finite(proj_res))) {
   251→              warning(sprintf("Permutation %d, Comp %d: Non-finite values after P3 projection. Replacing with 0.", perm_idx, current_a))
   252→              proj_res[!is.finite(proj_res)] <- 0
   253→          }
   254→          proj_res
   255→      } else {
   256→          Ea_perm
   257→      }
   258→      
   259→      modp <- run_pca_perm(Ea_perm_proj)
   260→      if (is.null(modp)) return(NA_real_)
   261→      
   262→      # Pass ... down to internal_stat_fun
   263→      do.call(internal_stat_fun, c(list(modp = modp, a = current_a), list(...)))
   264→  }
   265→
   266→  # ---------- Run Permutations ----------
   267→  Fq <- matrix(NA, nrow = nperm, ncol = comps)
   268→  n_complete <- rep(0, comps)
   269→  pvals <- rep(NA, comps)
   270→  comps_tested <- 0
   271→  
   272→  apply_fun <- if (parallel) future.apply::future_lapply else lapply
   273→  
   274→  message(sprintf("Running %d permutations sequentially for up to %d PCA components (alpha=%.3f, %s)...", 
   275→                  nperm, comps, alpha, if(parallel) "parallel" else "serial"))
   276→  
   277→  for (a in 1:comps) {
   278→      message(sprintf("  Testing Component %d/%d...", a, comps))
   279→      # Pass extra_args down via lapply's ...
   280→      perm_args <- list(X = seq_len(nperm), FUN = one_perm, current_a = a)
   281→      if (parallel) perm_args$future.seed <- TRUE
   282→      perm_args <- c(perm_args, extra_args)
   283→      
   284→      perm_vals_list <- do.call(apply_fun, perm_args)
   285→      perm_vals_a <- unlist(perm_vals_list)
   286→      Fq[, a] <- perm_vals_a
   287→      n_complete[a] <- sum(!is.na(perm_vals_a))
   288→      
   289→      if (n_complete[a] < nperm) {
   290→          warning(sprintf("Component %d: %d/%d permutations failed (e.g., SVD error). Using %d successful permutations.",
   291→                          a, nperm - n_complete[a], nperm, n_complete[a]))
   292→      }
   293→      if (n_complete[a] == 0) {
   294→          warning(sprintf("Component %d: All permutations failed. Cannot compute p-value.", a))
   295→          pvals[a] <- NA
   296→          comps_tested <- a
   297→          break # Stop if all perms failed
   298→      }
   299→      
   300→      # Calculate empirical p-value
   301→      obs_a <- observed_stats[a]
   302→      perm_vals_a_clean <- stats::na.omit(perm_vals_a)
   303→      if (is.na(obs_a)) {
   304→          pval_a <- NA
   305→          warning(sprintf("Component %d: Observed statistic is NA, cannot compute p-value.", a))
   306→      } else {
   307→          if (alternative == "greater") {
   308→              b <- sum(perm_vals_a_clean >= obs_a)
   309→              pval_a <- (b + 1) / (n_complete[a] + 1)
   310→          } else if (alternative == "less") {
   311→              b <- sum(perm_vals_a_clean <= obs_a)
   312→              pval_a <- (b + 1) / (n_complete[a] + 1)
   313→          } else { # two.sided
   314→              b_greater <- sum(perm_vals_a_clean >= obs_a)
   315→              b_less <- sum(perm_vals_a_clean <= obs_a)
   316→              pval_two <- 2 * min((b_greater + 1) / (n_complete[a] + 1), (b_less + 1) / (n_complete[a] + 1))
   317→              pval_a <- min(pval_two, 1.0)
   318→          }
   319→      }
   320→      pvals[a] <- pval_a
   321→      comps_tested <- a
   322→      
   323→      # Sequential stopping rule
   324→      if (!is.na(pval_a) && pval_a > alpha) {
   325→          message(sprintf("  Component %d p-value (%.4g) > alpha (%.3f). Stopping sequential testing.", a, pval_a, alpha))
   326→          break
   327→      }
   328→  } # End loop over components
   329→  
   330→  # ---------- Calculate CIs and final results table ----------
   331→  comp_df_list <- vector("list", comps_tested)
   332→  for (i in 1:comps_tested) {
   333→      lower_ci <- NA; upper_ci <- NA
   334→      if (n_complete[i] > 1) {
   335→          perm_vals_i_clean <- stats::na.omit(Fq[, i])
   336→          cis <- stats::quantile(perm_vals_i_clean, probs = c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE)
   337→          lower_ci <- cis[1]; upper_ci <- cis[2]
   338→      }
   339→      comp_df_list[[i]] <- tibble::tibble(
   340→          comp = i,
   341→          observed = observed_stats[i],
   342→          pval = pvals[i],
   343→          lower_ci = lower_ci,
   344→          upper_ci = upper_ci
   345→      )
   346→  }
   347→  component_results <- dplyr::bind_rows(comp_df_list)
   348→  
   349→  # ---------- Output Structure ----------
   350→  out <- list(
   351→      call = match.call(),
   352→      component_results = component_results,
   353→      perm_values = Fq[, 1:comps_tested, drop = FALSE],
   354→      alpha = alpha,
   355→      alternative = alternative,
   356→      method = sprintf("Permutation test for PCA (Vitale et al. 2017 P3) (statistic: %s, stepwise: %s, shuffle: %s)", 
   357→                       stat_name, stepwise, shuffle_type),
   358→      nperm = n_complete[1:comps_tested] # Report vector of successful permutations per component
   359→  )
   360→  class(out) <- c("perm_test_pca", "perm_test")
   361→  out
   362→}
   363→
   364→# Re-introducing specific reconstruct.pca method as the generic bi_projector one is not suitable
   365→#' Reconstruct Data from PCA Results
   366→#'
   367→#' Reconstructs the original (centered) data matrix from the PCA scores and loadings.
   368→#'
   369→#' @param x A `pca` object.
   370→#' @param comp Integer vector specifying which components to use for reconstruction (default: all components in `x`).
   371→#' @param ... Extra arguments (ignored).
   372→#' @return A matrix representing the reconstructed data in the *original* scale (preprocessing reversed).
   373→#' @export
   374→reconstruct.pca <- function(x, comp = 1:ncomp(x), ...) {
   375→  # Check component indices
   376→  chk::chk_vector(comp)
   377→  chk::chk_subset(comp, 1:ncomp(x))
   378→  
   379→  # Use standard PCA reconstruction: scores %*% t(loadings)
   380→  reconstructed_proc <- scores(x)[, comp, drop=FALSE] %*% t(coef(x)[, comp, drop=FALSE])
   381→  
   382→  # Reverse the preprocessing to return data in original scale
   383→  reverse_transform(x$preproc, reconstructed_proc)
   384→}

   387→# Ensure print.perm_test exists or define print.perm_test_pca
   388→# Assuming print.perm_test from discriminant_projector handles the structure:
   389→# list(statistic, perm_values, p.value, alternative, method, nperm, call)
   390→# Our structure is different (component_results data frame).
   391→# Let's define a specific print method.
   392→
   393→#' Print Method for perm_test_pca Objects
   394→#' 
   395→#' Provides a concise summary of the PCA permutation test results.
   396→#' 
   397→#' @param x An object of class `perm_test_pca`.
   398→#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to printing methods.
   399→#' @return Invisibly returns the input object `x`.
   400→#' @export
   401→print.perm_test_pca <- function(x, ...) {
   402→  cat("\nPCA Permutation Test Results\n\n")
   403→  cat("Method: ", x$method, "\n")
   404→  cat("Alternative: ", x$alternative, "\n")
   405→  cat("\nComponent Results:\n")
   406→  print(as.data.frame(x$component_results)) # Print the results table
   407→  cat("\nNumber of successful permutations per component:", paste(x$nperm, collapse=", "), "\n")
   408→  invisible(x)
   409→}

   412→
   413→#' Rotate PCA Loadings
   414→#'
   415→#' Apply a specified rotation to the component loadings of a PCA model. 
   416→#' This function leverages the GPArotation package to apply orthogonal 
   417→#' or oblique rotations.
   418→#'
   419→#' @param x A PCA model object, typically created using the `pca()` function.
   420→#' @param ncomp The number of components to rotate. Must be <= ncomp(x).
   421→#' @param type The type of rotation to apply. Supported rotation types:
   422→#'
   423→#' \describe{
   424→#'   \item{"varimax"}{Orthogonal Varimax rotation}
   425→#'   \item{"quartimax"}{Orthogonal Quartimax rotation}
   426→#'   \item{"promax"}{Oblique Promax rotation}
   427→#' }
   428→#'
   429→#' @param loadings_type For oblique rotations, which loadings to use:
   430→#'
   431→#' \describe{
   432→#'   \item{"pattern"}{Use pattern loadings as \code{v}}
   433→#'   \item{"structure"}{Use structure loadings (\code{pattern_loadings \%*\% Phi}) as \code{v}}
   434→#' }
   435→#'
   436→#' Ignored for orthogonal rotations.
   437→#'
   438→#' @param score_method How to recompute scores after rotation:
   439→#'
   440→#' \describe{
   441→#'   \item{"auto"}{For orthogonal rotations, use 
   442→#'     \code{scores_new = scores_original \%*\% t(R)}} 
   443→#'     For oblique rotations, recompute from the pseudoinverse.
   444→#'
   445→#'   \item{"recompute"}{Always recompute scores from \code{X_proc} and 
   446→#'     the pseudoinverse of rotated loadings.}
   447→#'
   448→#'   \item{"original"}{For orth rotations, same as \code{auto}, 
   449→#'     but may not work for oblique rotations.}
   450→#' }
   451→#'
   452→#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to GPArotation functions.
   453→#'
   454→#' @return A modified PCA object with class \code{rotated_pca} and additional fields:
   455→#' \describe{
   456→#'   \item{v}{Rotated loadings}
   457→#'   \item{s}{Rotated scores}
   458→#'   \item{sdev}{Updated standard deviations of rotated components}
   459→#'   \item{explained_variance}{Proportion of explained variance for each rotated component}
   460→#'   \item{rotation}{A list with rotation details: \code{type}, \code{R} (orth) or \code{Phi} (oblique), and \code{loadings_type}}
   461→#' }
   462→#'
   463→#' @importFrom GPArotation GPForth GPFoblq
   464→#' @export
   465→#'
   466→#' @examples
   467→#' # Perform PCA on the iris dataset
   468→#' data(iris)
   469→#' X <- as.matrix(iris[,1:4])
   470→#' res <- pca(X, ncomp=4)
   471→#'
   472→#' # Apply varimax rotation to the first 3 components
   473→#' rotated_res <- rotate(res, ncomp=3, type="varimax")
   474→rotate.pca <- function(x, ncomp, type=c("varimax", "quartimax", "promax"),
   475→                       loadings_type=c("pattern", "structure"),
   476→                       score_method=c("auto", "recompute", "original"),
   477→                       ...) {
   478→  type <- match.arg(type)
   479→  loadings_type <- match.arg(loadings_type)
   480→  score_method <- match.arg(score_method)
   481→  
   482→  if (!requireNamespace("GPArotation", quietly = TRUE)) {
   483→    stop("GPArotation package is required for rotations. Please install it.")
   484→  }
   485→  
   486→  if (ncomp > ncomp(x)) {
   487→    stop("ncomp cannot exceed the number of available components in 'x'.")
   488→  }
   489→  
   490→  # Extract loadings and scores for the specified components
   491→  loadings_to_rotate <- x$v[, 1:ncomp, drop=FALSE]
   492→  scores_original <- x$s[, 1:ncomp, drop=FALSE]
   493→  
   494→  # We'll need X_proc to recompute scores in 'recompute' mode or for oblique rotations
   495→  # If we don't have X_proc directly, we can reconstruct it:
   496→  # X_proc ≈ scores_original %*% t(loadings_to_rotate)
   497→  # This relies on the PCA model: X_proc = s * v', assuming preproc applied.
   498→  
   499→  # Just in case we need full pre-processed data:
   500→  # For 'recompute' or oblique rotations, we must have a stable way to get X_proc.
   501→  # We know: X_proc = scores_original %*% t(loadings_to_rotate)
   502→  
   503→  # Create loadings object for GPArotation
   504→  L <- loadings_to_rotate
   505→  class(L) <- "loadings"
   506→  
   507→  # Perform rotation
   508→  if (type %in% c("varimax", "quartimax")) {
   509→    # Orthogonal rotation
   510→    rot_res <- GPArotation::GPForth(L, method=type, ...)
   511→    rotated_loadings <- rot_res$loadings
   512→    R <- rot_res$Th  # rotation matrix
   513→    
   514→    # Compute scores_new:
   515→    # Depending on score_method:
   516→    if (score_method == "auto" || score_method == "original") {
   517→      # For orth rotations: scores_new = scores_original %*% t(R)
   518→      scores_new <- scores_original %*% t(R)
   519→    } else if (score_method == "recompute") {
   520→      # recompute from X_proc:
   521→      X_proc <- scores_original %*% t(loadings_to_rotate)
   522→      inv_rotated <- corpcor::pseudoinverse(rotated_loadings)
   523→      scores_new <- X_proc %*% inv_rotated
   524→    }
   525→    
   526→    # Update sdev and explained variance
   527→    variances <- apply(scores_new, 2, stats::var)
   528→    sdev_new <- sqrt(variances)
   529→    explained_variance <- variances / sum(variances)
   530→    
   531→    # Update object
   532→    x$v[, 1:ncomp] <- as.matrix(rotated_loadings)
   533→    x$s[, 1:ncomp] <- scores_new
   534→    x$sdev[1:ncomp] <- sdev_new
   535→    x$explained_variance <- explained_variance
   536→    
   537→    x$rotation <- list(type=type, loadings_type="N/A (orthogonal)", R=R, Phi=NULL)
   538→    
   539→  } else {
   540→    # Oblique rotation
   541→    rot_res <- GPArotation::GPFoblq(L, method=type, ...)
   542→    pattern_loadings <- rot_res$loadings
   543→    Phi <- rot_res$Phi
   544→    
   545→    # Choose loadings based on loadings_type
   546→    if (loadings_type == "pattern") {
   547→      chosen_loadings <- pattern_loadings
   548→    } else {
   549→      # structure loadings = pattern_loadings %*% Phi
   550→      chosen_loadings <- pattern_loadings %*% Phi
   551→    }
   552→    
   553→    # Compute scores_new:
   554→    # For oblique rotations, if score_method == "original" doesn't make sense because original was orth-based.
   555→    # We'll handle "original" by warning or just do what "auto" does for oblique (which is recompute).
   556→    
   557→    if (score_method == "original") {
   558→      warning("For oblique rotations, 'original' score_method is not valid. Using 'auto'.")
   559→      score_method <- "auto"
   560→    }
   561→    
   562→    if (score_method == "auto") {
   563→      # auto = oblique => recompute from pseudoinverse
   564→      X_proc <- scores_original %*% t(loadings_to_rotate)
   565→      inv_chosen <- corpcor::pseudoinverse(chosen_loadings)
   566→      scores_new <- X_proc %*% inv_chosen
   567→    } else if (score_method == "recompute") {
   568→      # Same as above
   569→      X_proc <- scores_original %*% t(loadings_to_rotate)
   570→      inv_chosen <- corpcor::pseudoinverse(chosen_loadings)
   571→      scores_new <- X_proc %*% inv_chosen
   572→    }
   573→    
   574→    # Update sdev and explained variance
   575→    variances <- apply(scores_new, 2, stats::var)
   576→    sdev_new <- sqrt(variances)
   577→    explained_variance <- variances / sum(variances)
   578→    
   579→    # Update object
   580→    x$v[, 1:ncomp] <- chosen_loadings
   581→    x$s[, 1:ncomp] <- scores_new
   582→    x$sdev[1:ncomp] <- sdev_new
   583→    x$explained_variance <- explained_variance
   584→    
   585→    x$rotation <- list(type=type, loadings_type=loadings_type, R=NULL, Phi=Phi)
   586→  }
   587→  
   588→  # Add rotated_pca class
   589→  if (!("rotated_pca" %in% class(x))) {
   590→    class(x) <- c("rotated_pca", class(x))
   591→  }
   592→  
   593→  x
   594→}
   595→
   596→
   597→
   598→
   599→#' Biplot for PCA Objects (Enhanced with ggrepel)
   600→#'
   601→#' Creates a 2D biplot for a \code{pca} object, using \pkg{ggplot2} and \pkg{ggrepel} 
   602→#' to show both sample scores (observations) and variable loadings (arrows).
   603→#'
   604→#' @param x A \code{pca} object returned by \code{\link{pca}}.
   605→#' @param y (ignored) Placeholder to match \code{biplot(x, y, ...)} signature.
   606→#' @param dims A length-2 integer vector specifying which principal components to plot 
   607→#'   on the x and y axes. Defaults to \code{c(1, 2)}.
   608→#' @param scale_arrows A numeric factor to scale the variable loadings (arrows). Default is 2.
   609→#' @param alpha_points Transparency level for the sample points. Default is 0.6.
   610→#' @param point_size Size for the sample points. Default is 2.
   611→#' @param point_labels Optional character vector of labels for the sample points. 
   612→#'   If \code{NULL}, rownames of the scores matrix are used if available; otherwise numeric indices.
   613→#' @param var_labels Optional character vector of variable names (columns in the original data). 
   614→#'   If \code{NULL}, rownames of \code{x\$v} are used if available; otherwise "Var1", "Var2", etc.
   615→#' @param arrow_color Color for the loading arrows. Default is "red".
   616→#' @param text_color Color for the variable label text. Default is "red".
   617→#' @param repel_points Logical; if TRUE, repel sample labels using \code{geom_text_repel}. Default is \code{TRUE}.
   618→#' @param repel_vars Logical; if TRUE, repel variable labels using \code{geom_text_repel}. Default is \code{FALSE}.
   619→#' @param ... Additional arguments passed on to \code{ggplot2} or \code{ggrepel} functions (if needed).
   620→#'
   621→#' @details
   622→#' This function constructs a scatterplot of the PCA scores (observations) on two chosen components
   623→#' and overlays arrows for the loadings (variables). The arrow length and direction indicate how each
   624→#' variable contributes to those principal components. You can control arrow scaling with \code{scale_arrows}.
   625→#'
   626→#' If your \code{pca} object includes an \code{$explained_variance} field (e.g., proportion of variance per component),
   627→#' those values will appear in the axis labels. Otherwise, the axes are labeled simply as "PC1", "PC2", etc.
   628→#'
   629→#' **Note**: If you do not have \pkg{ggrepel} installed, you can set \code{repel_points=FALSE} and 
   630→#' \code{repel_vars=FALSE}, or install \pkg{ggrepel}.
   631→#'
   632→#' @return A \code{ggplot} object.
   633→#'
   634→#' @import ggplot2
   635→#' @importFrom ggrepel geom_text_repel
   636→#' @export
   637→#'
   638→#' @examples
   639→#' \dontrun{
   640→#' data(iris)
   641→#' X <- as.matrix(iris[,1:4])
   642→#' pca_res <- pca(X, ncomp=2)
   643→#'
   644→#' # Enhanced biplot with repelled text
   645→#' biplot(pca_res, repel_points=TRUE, repel_vars=TRUE)
   646→#' }
   647→biplot.pca <- function(x,
   648→                       y = NULL,  # ignored, matching biplot generic
   649→                       dims = c(1, 2),
   650→                       scale_arrows = 2,
   651→                       alpha_points = 0.6,
   652→                       point_size = 2,
   653→                       point_labels = NULL,
   654→                       var_labels = NULL,
   655→                       arrow_color = "red",
   656→                       text_color = "red",
   657→                       repel_points = TRUE,
   658→                       repel_vars = FALSE,
   659→                       ...) 
   660→{
   661→  # Check dims
   662→  n_comps <- ncomp(x)
   663→  if (any(dims > n_comps)) {
   664→    stop("Requested components exceed the number of available components in 'x'.")
   665→  }
   666→  if (length(dims) != 2) {
   667→    stop("'dims' must be a vector of length 2, e.g. c(1, 2).")
   668→  }
   669→  
   670→  # Extract scores and loadings
   671→  sc <- x$s  # observations x components
   672→  ld <- x$v  # variables x components
   673→  if (is.null(sc) || is.null(ld)) {
   674→    stop("The PCA object does not contain both scores (x$s) and loadings (x$v).")
   675→  }
   676→  
   677→  # Subset to dims
   678→  sc2 <- sc[, dims, drop = FALSE]
   679→  ld2 <- ld[, dims, drop = FALSE]
   680→  
   681→  # If we have explained variance, use it
   682→  if (!is.null(x$explained_variance)) {
   683→    pc_var <- x$explained_variance
   684→  } else {
   685→    pc_var <- rep(NA_real_, ncol(sc))
   686→  }
   687→  
   688→  # Convert scores to data.frame
   689→  scores_df <- as.data.frame(sc2)
   690→  colnames(scores_df) <- c("PCx", "PCy")
   691→  
   692→  # Assign labels to points
   693→  if (is.null(point_labels)) {
   694→    if (!is.null(rownames(sc))) {
   695→      point_labels <- rownames(sc)
   696→    } else {
   697→      point_labels <- seq_len(nrow(sc))
   698→    }
   699→  }
   700→  scores_df$labels <- point_labels
   701→  
   702→  # Convert loadings to data.frame
   703→  loadings_df <- as.data.frame(ld2)
   704→  colnames(loadings_df) <- c("PCx", "PCy")
   705→  
   706→  # Assign variable labels
   707→  if (is.null(var_labels)) {
   708→    if (!is.null(rownames(ld))) {
   709→      var_labels <- rownames(ld)
   710→    } else {
   711→      var_labels <- paste0("Var", seq_len(nrow(ld)))
   712→    }
   713→  }
   714→  loadings_df$var <- var_labels
   715→  
   716→  # Scale loadings for arrow length
   717→  loadings_df$PCx <- loadings_df$PCx * scale_arrows
   718→  loadings_df$PCy <- loadings_df$PCy * scale_arrows
   719→  
   720→  # Build axis labels (include % variance if available)
   721→  pcx_lab <- if (!is.na(pc_var[dims[1]])) {
   722→    paste0("PC", dims[1], " (", round(100 * pc_var[dims[1]], 1), "%)")
   723→  } else {
   724→    paste0("PC", dims[1])
   725→  }
   726→  pcy_lab <- if (!is.na(pc_var[dims[2]])) {
   727→    paste0("PC", dims[2], " (", round(100 * pc_var[dims[2]], 1), "%)")
   728→  } else {
   729→    paste0("PC", dims[2])
   730→  }
   731→  
   732→  # Start building the plot
   733→  plt <- ggplot(scores_df, aes(x = .data$PCx, y = .data$PCy)) +
   734→    geom_point(alpha = alpha_points, size = point_size, color = "blue") +
   735→    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
   736→    coord_equal() +
   737→    xlab(pcx_lab) +
   738→    ylab(pcy_lab)
   739→  
   740→  # Add text labels for points
   741→  # Use ggrepel if repel_points=TRUE and ggrepel is installed
   742→  # fallback to geom_text if not installed or repel_points=FALSE
   743→  can_repel <- requireNamespace("ggrepel", quietly = TRUE)
   744→  
   745→  if (repel_points && can_repel) {
   746→    plt <- plt + 
   747→      ggrepel::geom_text_repel(aes(label = .data$labels), color = "black", size = 3, ...)
   748→  } else {
   749→    plt <- plt + 
   750→      geom_text(aes(label = .data$labels), hjust = 1.1, vjust = 0.5, color = "black", size = 3, ...)
   751→  }
   752→  
   753→  # Add loadings arrows
   754→  plt <- plt +
   755→    geom_segment(data = loadings_df,
   756→                 aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = .data$PCx, yend = .data$PCy),
   757→                 arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.02, "npc")),
   758→                 color = arrow_color,
   759→                 size = 0.7)
   760→  
   761→  # Add variable names near arrow tips
   762→  if (repel_vars && can_repel) {
   763→    plt <- plt +
   764→      ggrepel::geom_text_repel(data = loadings_df,
   765→                               aes(x = .data$PCx, y = .data$PCy, label = .data$var),
   766→                               color = text_color,
   767→                               size = 3,
   768→                               ...)
   769→  } else {
   770→    plt <- plt +
   771→      geom_text(data = loadings_df,
   772→                aes(x = .data$PCx, y = .data$PCy, label = .data$var),
   773→                color = text_color, vjust = -0.5, size = 3,
   774→                ...)
   775→  }
   776→  
   777→  plt
   778→}
   779→
   780→#' Print Method for PCA Objects
   781→#'
   782→#' Provide a color-enhanced summary of the PCA object, including 
   783→#' dimensions, variance explained, and a quick component breakdown.
   784→#'
   785→#' @param x A \code{pca} object.
   786→#' @param ... Ignored (for compatibility).
   787→#' @export
   788→print.pca <- function(x, ...) {
   789→  cat(
   790→    crayon::bold(crayon::green("PCA object")),
   791→    " -- derived from SVD\n\n"
   792→  )
   793→  
   794→  # Basic dims
   795→  nobs <- nrow(x$s)         # number of observations
   796→  nvars <- nrow(x$v)        # number of variables
   797→  ncomp_used <- ncol(x$s)   # how many comps we actually have
   798→  
   799→  cat(crayon::cyan("Data: "), nobs, " observations x ", nvars, " variables\n", sep = "")
   800→  cat(crayon::cyan("Components retained: "), ncomp_used, "\n\n", sep = "")
   801→  
   802→  # Possibly show proportion of variance if available
   803→  if (!is.null(x$sdev)) {
   804→    eigenvals <- x$sdev^2
   805→    prop_var <- eigenvals / sum(eigenvals)
   806→    cum_var <- cumsum(prop_var)
   807→    
   808→    cat(crayon::bold("Variance explained (per component):\n"))
   809→    cat(
   810→      formatC(seq_len(ncomp_used), width = 2), " ",
   811→      formatC(round(prop_var * 100, 2), width=6), "%  ",
   812→      "(cumulative: ",
   813→      formatC(round(cum_var * 100, 2), width=6), "%)\n",
   814→      sep = ""
   815→    )
   816→    cat("\n")
   817→  }
   818→  
   819→  # If we have an explained_variance field (like from rotate), use that
   820→  if (!is.null(x$explained_variance)) {
   821→    cat(crayon::bold("Explained variance from rotation:\n"))
   822→    cat(paste(round(x$explained_variance * 100, 2), "%\n"), "\n\n")
   823→  }
   824→  
   825→  # Possibly show info about rotation
   826→  if (!is.null(x$rotation)) {
   827→    cat(crayon::bold("Rotation details:\n"))
   828→    cat("  Type:", x$rotation$type, "\n")
   829→    if (!is.null(x$rotation$loadings_type)) {
   830→      cat("  Loadings type:", x$rotation$loadings_type, "\n")
   831→    }
   832→    cat("\n")
   833→  }
   834→  
   835→  invisible(x)
   836→}
   837→
   838→
   839→#' Screeplot for PCA
   840→#'
   841→#' Displays the variance explained by each principal component as a bar or line plot.
   842→#' 
   843→#' @param x A \code{pca} object.
   844→#' @param type "barplot" or "lines".
   845→#' @param main Plot title.
   846→#' @param ... Additional args to pass to base R plotting.
   847→#' @export
   848→screeplot.pca <- function(x, type="barplot", main="Screeplot", ...) {
   849→  pc_variance <- x$sdev^2
   850→  percent_variance <- pc_variance / sum(pc_variance) * 100
   851→  
   852→  if (type == "barplot") {
   853→    graphics::barplot(percent_variance, 
   854→            xlab="Principal Component",
   855→            ylab="Percentage of Variance Explained",
   856→            names.arg=paste0("PC", 1:length(percent_variance)),
   857→            main=main, ...)
   858→  } else if (type == "lines") {
   859→    plot(seq_len(length(percent_variance)), percent_variance, type="o",
   860→         xlab="Component",
   861→         ylab="Proportion of Variance",
   862→         main=main, ...)
   863→  }
   864→  invisible(NULL)
   865→}
   866→
   867→
   868→#' PCA Outlier Diagnostics
   869→#'
   870→#' Calculates Hotelling T^2 (score distance) and Q-residual (orthogonal distance)
   871→#' for each observation, given a chosen number of components.
   872→#'
   873→#' @param x A \code{pca} object.
   874→#' @param X The original data matrix used for PCA.
   875→#' @param ncomp Number of components to consider.
   876→#' @param cutoff Logical or numeric specifying threshold for labeling outliers. If \code{TRUE},
   877→#'   uses some typical statistical threshold (F-dist) for T^2, or sets an arbitrary Q limit.
   878→#'   If numeric, treat it as a cutoff. Default is \code{FALSE} (no labeling).
   879→#' @return A data frame with columns \code{T2} and \code{Q}, and optionally an outlier flag.
   880→#' @export
   881→pca_outliers <- function(x, X, ncomp, cutoff=FALSE) {
   882→  # compute T2 (score distances)
   883→  T2 <- score_distances.pca(x, ncomp, xorig=X)
   884→  T2vals <- T2[, ncomp]
   885→  
   886→  # compute Q (residual distances)
   887→  Q <- orth_distances.pca(x, ncomp, xorig=X)
   888→  Qvals <- Q[, ncomp]
   889→  
   890→  # optional outlier logic...
   891→  
   892→  data.frame(T2 = T2vals, Q = Qvals)
   893→}
