#' Create a Multiblock Projector
#'
#' Constructs a multiblock projector using the given component matrix (`v`), a preprocessing function, and a list of block indices. 
#' This allows for the projection of multiblock data, where each block represents a different set of variables or features.
#'
#' @param v A matrix of components with dimensions `nrow(v)` by `ncol(v)` (columns = number of components).
#' @param preproc A pre-processing function for the data (default: `prep(pass())`).
#' @param block_indices A list of numeric vectors specifying the indices of each data block.
#' @param classes (optional) A character vector specifying additional class attributes of the object, default is NULL.
#' @param ... Extra arguments.
#' @return A `multiblock_projector` object.
#'
#' @seealso projector
#' @export
#' @examples
#' # Generate some example data
#' X1 <- matrix(rnorm(10 * 5), 10, 5)
#' X2 <- matrix(rnorm(10 * 5), 10, 5)
#' X <- cbind(X1, X2)
#'
#' # Compute PCA on the combined data
#' pc <- pca(X, ncomp = 8)
#'
#' # Create a multiblock projector using PCA components and block indices
#' mb_proj <- multiblock_projector(pc$v, block_indices = list(1:5, 6:10))
#'
#' # Project multiblock data using the multiblock projector
#' mb_scores <- project(mb_proj, X)
multiblock_projector <- function(v, preproc=prep(pass()), ..., block_indices, classes=NULL) {
  chk::chk_list(block_indices)
  sumind <- sum(sapply(block_indices, length))
  chk::chk_equal(sumind, nrow(v))
  
  projector(v, preproc, block_indices=block_indices, ..., classes=c(classes, "multiblock_projector"))
}


#' Create a Multiblock Bi-Projector
#'
#' Constructs a multiblock bi-projector using the given component matrix (`v`), score matrix (`s`), singular values (`sdev`),
#' a preprocessing function, and a list of block indices. This allows for two-way mapping with multiblock data.
#'
#' @param v A matrix of components (nrow = number of variables, ncol = number of components).
#' @param s A matrix of scores (nrow = samples, ncol = components).
#' @param sdev A numeric vector of singular values or standard deviations.
#' @param preproc A pre-processing object (default: `prep(pass())`).
#' @param block_indices A list of numeric vectors specifying data block variable indices.
#' @param classes Additional class attributes (default NULL).
#' @param ... Extra arguments.
#' @return A `multiblock_biprojector` object.
#'
#' @seealso bi_projector, multiblock_projector
#' @export
multiblock_biprojector <- function(v, s, sdev, preproc=prep(pass()), ..., block_indices, classes=NULL) {
  sumind <- sum(sapply(block_indices, length))
  chk::chk_equal(sumind, nrow(v))
  bi_projector(v, s=s, sdev=sdev, preproc=preproc, block_indices=block_indices, ..., classes=c(classes, "multiblock_biprojector", "multiblock_projector"))
}


#' Extract the Block Indices from a Multiblock Projector
#'
#' @param x A `multiblock_projector` object.
#' @param i Ignored.
#' @param ... Ignored.
#' @return The list of block indices.
#' @export
block_indices.multiblock_projector <- function(x,i,...) {
  x$block_indices
}

#' @export
block_lengths.multiblock_projector <- function(x) {
  sapply(block_indices(x), length)
}

#' @export
nblocks.multiblock_projector <- function(x) {
  length(block_indices(x))
}

#' Project Data onto a Specific Block
#'
#' Projects the new data onto the subspace defined by a specific block of variables.
#'
#' @param x A `multiblock_projector` object.
#' @param new_data The new data to be projected.
#' @param block The block index (1-based) to project onto.
#' @param least_squares Logical. If `TRUE` (default), use least squares projection.
#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to `partial_project`.
#' @return The projected scores for the specified block.
#' @export
project_block.multiblock_projector <- function(x, new_data, block,least_squares=TRUE, ...) {
  # Check block validity
  nb <- nblocks(x)
  if (block < 1 || block > nb) {
    stop("Block index out of range.")
  }
  
  ind <- block_indices(x)[[block]]
  partial_project(x, new_data, colind=ind, least_squares,...)
}

#' Coefficients for a Multiblock Projector
#'
#' Extracts the components (loadings) for a given block or the entire projector.
#'
#' @param object A `multiblock_projector` object.
#' @param block Optional block index. If missing, returns loadings for all variables.
#' @param ... Additional arguments.
#' @return A matrix of loadings.
#' @export
coef.multiblock_projector <- function(object, block,...) {
  if (missing(block)) {
    # Instead of NextMethod(object), just use NextMethod() to call coef.projector
    NextMethod()
  } else {
    nb <- nblocks(object)
    if (block < 1 || block > nb) {
      stop("Block index out of range.")
    }
    ind <- object$block_indices[[block]]
    object$v[ind,,drop=FALSE]
  }
}

#' Pretty Print Method for `multiblock_biprojector` Objects
#'
#' Display a summary of a `multiblock_biprojector` object.
#'
#' @param x A `multiblock_biprojector` object.
#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to `print()`.
#' @return Invisible `multiblock_biprojector` object.
#' @export
print.multiblock_biprojector <- function(x, ...) {
  cat("Multiblock Bi-Projector object:\n")
  cat("  Projection matrix dimensions: ", nrow(x$v), "x", ncol(x$v), "\n")
  
  # Print block indices in a nicer format
  cat("  Block indices:\n")
  lapply(seq_along(x$block_indices), function(i) {
    cat("    Block ", i, ": ", paste(x$block_indices[[i]], collapse = ","), "\n", sep="")
  })
  
  invisible(x)
}

# -------------------------------------------------------------
# Default multiblock shuffle helper
# -------------------------------------------------------------
#' Shuffle rows of a matrix or list of matrices
#'
#' Helper used internally by permutation tests to randomize the
#' observation order of either a single matrix or each matrix in a
#' list.  Each block (or the single matrix) has its rows permuted
#' independently.
#' @param data A matrix or list of matrices with the same number of rows.
#' @keywords internal
#' @noRd
.default_multiblock_shuffle <- function(data) {
  if (is.list(data)) {
    lapply(data, function(M) M[sample(nrow(M)), , drop = FALSE])
  } else if (is.matrix(data)) {
    data[sample(nrow(data)), , drop = FALSE]
  } else {
    stop("Unsupported data type for multiblock shuffling")
  }
}





#' @importFrom stats var
#' @importFrom RSpectra svds
#' @importFrom future.apply future_lapply
#' @export
perm_test.multiblock_biprojector <- function(
    x,
    Xlist          = NULL,          # optional list of blocks
    nperm          = 500,
    comps          = 4,             # max # components to test
    alpha          = 0.05,
    shuffle_fun    = NULL,          # custom shuffler
    parallel       = FALSE,
    alternative    = c("greater","less","two.sided"),
    use_rspectra   = TRUE,
    ...
){
  alternative <- match.arg(alternative)

  ## ------------------------------------------------------------------
  ## 1.  pull block indices & component count
  ## ------------------------------------------------------------------
  blk_ind   <- block_indices(x)
  B         <- length(blk_ind)
  Kmax      <- ncol(x$v)                    # components available
  comps     <- min(comps, Kmax)
  if (comps < 1L) stop("Need at least one component to test.")

  n         <- nrow(scores(x))

  ## ------------------------------------------------------------------
  ## 2.  helper  -  get block-wise score matrix  (n x B)  for comp k
  ## ------------------------------------------------------------------
  get_Tk <- function(comp_k, data_list = NULL, S_perm = NULL){
      if (is.null(data_list)){          # use stored scores — fast
          # If S_perm is provided (already shuffled full score matrix), use it
          # Otherwise, use the original scores (only for calculating observed stat)
          sc_matrix <- if (!is.null(S_perm)) S_perm else scores(x)
          sc <- sc_matrix[ , comp_k, drop = FALSE]   # n × 1
          # Return a matrix with B identical columns of these scores
          do.call(cbind, replicate(B, sc, simplify = FALSE))
      } else {                          # re‑project if user gave data
          # Project each block in data_list onto component k using original model x
          lapply(seq_len(B), function(b){
              xb <- data_list[[b]]
              # project block b onto *single* component k
              nd_proc <- apply_transform(x$preproc, xb, blk_ind[[b]])
              v_sub   <- x$v[ blk_ind[[b]] , comp_k , drop = FALSE]
              as.vector(nd_proc %*% v_sub)
          }) |> do.call(cbind, args = _) # Result is n x B matrix
      }
  }

  ## ------------------------------------------------------------------
  ## 3.  statistic per component  –  leading eigenvalue of  TᵀT
  ## ------------------------------------------------------------------
  comp_stat <- function(Tk){
      S <- crossprod(Tk)                       # B × B
      if (B == 1) return(sum(Tk^2))            # trivial 1‑block case
      if (use_rspectra && requireNamespace("RSpectra", quietly = TRUE))
           RSpectra::svds(S, k = 1, nu = 0, nv = 1)$d[1]
      else eigen(S, symmetric = TRUE, only.values = TRUE)$values[1]
  }

  ## observed statistics ------------------------------------------------
  T_list_obs <- lapply(seq_len(comps), function(k){
      comp_stat( get_Tk(k, Xlist) )
  })
  obs_vec <- unlist(T_list_obs)

  ## ------------------------------------------------------------------
  ## 4.  decide what data to shuffle and set default shuffle
  ## ------------------------------------------------------------------
  if (is.null(Xlist)) {
      # Shuffle the scores matrix once per permutation
      data_to_shuffle            <- scores(x)
      use_shuffled_scores_matrix <- TRUE
  } else {
      if (length(Xlist) != B)
         stop("Length of Xlist must equal number of blocks in the model.")
      data_to_shuffle            <- Xlist
      use_shuffled_scores_matrix <- FALSE
  }

  if (is.null(shuffle_fun))
      shuffle_fun <- .default_multiblock_shuffle

  ## ------------------------------------------------------------------
  ## 5.  permutation loop (sequential stopping like PCA)
  ## ------------------------------------------------------------------
  Fperm   <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = nperm, ncol = comps)
  n_ok    <- integer(comps)

  apply_fun <- if (parallel) future.apply::future_lapply else lapply
  if (parallel) message("perm_test.multiblock_biprojector : permutations in parallel …")

  # Pre-calculate observed stats outside loop
  obs_stats <- sapply(seq_len(comps), function(k) {
    Tk_obs <- get_Tk(k, Xlist) # Use original Xlist or scores
    comp_stat(Tk_obs)
  })

  for (k in seq_len(comps)){ # Loop over components for sequential testing
      perm_fun <- function(i){ # Function for one permutation
          # Shuffle either the original scores matrix OR the list of X blocks
          data_perm <- shuffle_fun(data_to_shuffle)
          
          # Calculate statistic for component k based on shuffled data
          if (use_shuffled_scores_matrix) {
              # Pass the shuffled full score matrix (data_perm) to get_Tk
              Tk_perm <- get_Tk(comp_k = k, S_perm = data_perm)
          } else {
              # Pass the shuffled list of X blocks (data_perm) to get_Tk for re-projection
              Tk_perm <- get_Tk(comp_k = k, data_list = data_perm)
          }
          comp_stat(Tk_perm)
      }
      
      # Run permutations for component k
      plist   <- apply_fun(seq_len(nperm), perm_fun)
      perm_vals_k <- unlist(plist)
      Fperm[, k] <- perm_vals_k # Store results for component k
      n_ok[k]    <- sum(is.finite(perm_vals_k))

      ## empirical p‑value for component k
      obs_k <- obs_stats[k]
      g      <- sum(perm_vals_k >= obs_k, na.rm = TRUE)
      l      <- sum(perm_vals_k <= obs_k, na.rm = TRUE)
      pval   <- switch(alternative,
                       greater   = (g + 1)/(n_ok[k] + 1),
                       less      = (l + 1)/(n_ok[k] + 1),
                       two.sided = min(1, 2*min((g + 1)/(n_ok[k]+1),
                                                (l + 1)/(n_ok[k]+1))))
      if (!is.na(pval) && pval > alpha){          # Vitale‑like stop rule
          comps_tested <- k
          Fperm <- Fperm[, seq_len(comps_tested), drop = FALSE]
          n_ok  <- n_ok[seq_len(comps_tested)]
          obs_stats <- obs_stats[seq_len(comps_tested)]
          break # Stop testing further components
      }
      comps_tested <- k # Record that this component was tested
  }# end loop over components
  
  # Rename obs_vec to obs_stats to avoid clash
  obs_vec <- obs_stats 

  ## ------------------------------------------------------------------
  ## 6.  tidy return object  (same shape as perm_test.pca())
  ## ------------------------------------------------------------------
  comp_df <- data.frame(comp      = seq_along(obs_vec),
                        observed  = obs_vec,
                        pval      = sapply(seq_along(obs_vec), function(j){
                                         g <- sum(Fperm[,j] >= obs_vec[j], na.rm = TRUE)
                                         (g + 1)/(n_ok[j] + 1)}),
                        lower_ci  = apply(Fperm, 2, quantile, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
                        upper_ci  = apply(Fperm, 2, quantile, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE))

  structure(
     list(call              = match.call(),
          component_results = comp_df,
          perm_values       = Fperm,
          alpha             = alpha,
          alternative       = alternative,
          method            = sprintf(
              "Permutation test for multiblock consensus (stat = leading‑eigenvalue, %d blocks)", B),
          nperm             = n_ok),
     class = c("perm_test_multiblock","perm_test")
  )
}



#' @export
perm_test.multiblock_projector <- function(x,
                                           Xlist,
                                           nperm       = 500,
                                           comps       = 4,
                                           shuffle_fun = NULL,
                                           measure_fun = NULL,
                                           parallel    = FALSE,
                                           alternative = c("greater", "less",
                                                           "two.sided"),
                                           alpha       = 0.05,
                                           ...) {

  ## ---------- safety checks ----------
  if (is.null(Xlist)) 
    stop("Xlist is required for perm_test.multiblock_projector")
  if (!is.list(Xlist))
    stop("Xlist must be a list of block matrices.")

  B <- nblocks(x)
  if (length(Xlist) != B)
    stop("Length(Xlist) (", length(Xlist),
         ") differs from nblocks(x) (", B, ").")

  blk_idx <- block_indices(x)
  p_each  <- sapply(blk_idx, length)
  for (b in seq_len(B))
    if (ncol(Xlist[[b]]) != p_each[b])
      stop("Block ", b, ": #cols in Xlist (", ncol(Xlist[[b]]), 
           ") does not match block_indices (", p_each[b], ").")

  N <- nrow(Xlist[[1]])
  if (any(sapply(Xlist, nrow) != N))
    stop("All blocks must have the same number of rows.")

  Kmax <- min(comps, ncol(x$v))
  alternative <- match.arg(alternative)

  ## ---------- helpers ----------
  # block‑specific preprocessing (reuse x$preproc) ----
  prep_all <- function(Xl) {
    # Concatenate, apply transform to full matrix, then split back
    p_all <- sum(p_each)
    Xall <- matrix(0.0, nrow=N, ncol=p_all) # Pre-allocate
    cidx <- 1
    for(b in 1:B) {
      Xall[, cidx:(cidx+p_each[b]-1)] <- Xl[[b]]
      cidx <- cidx + p_each[b]
    }
    Xproc_all <- apply_transform(x$preproc, Xall)
    
    # Split back into list
    Xp_list <- vector("list", B)
    cidx <- 1
    for(b in 1:B) {
      Xp_list[[b]] <- Xproc_all[, cidx:(cidx+p_each[b]-1), drop=FALSE]
      cidx <- cidx + p_each[b]
    }
    Xp_list
  }

  # compute block scores for first K comps using original model 'x' ----
  get_block_scores <- function(Xp_list, K) {
    v      <- coef(x)        # Use original model's loadings (p_tot × d)
    vlist  <- lapply(seq_len(B), function(b)
                     v[ blk_idx[[b]], 1:K, drop = FALSE ])
    mapply(`%*%`, Xp_list, vlist, SIMPLIFY = FALSE)  # list of (N × K) matrices
  }

  # default statistic: mean |corr| across block pairs ----
  default_measure <- function(Scores_list, K) {
    # Scores_list is a list (length B) of (N x K) score matrices
    out <- numeric(K)
    if (B < 2) return(rep(NA_real_, K)) # Correlation requires at least 2 blocks
    for (k in seq_len(K)) {
      corrs <- combn(B, 2, FUN = function(idx) {
        # Ensure scores are vectors for cor()
        score1 <- Scores_list[[idx[1]]][, k]
        score2 <- Scores_list[[idx[2]]][, k]
        if (stats::var(score1) < .Machine$double.eps || stats::var(score2) < .Machine$double.eps) {
            # Return NA or 0 if variance is zero to avoid cor error/NA
            return(NA_real_) 
        } else {
            stats::cor(score1, score2)
        }
      })
      # Handle potential NAs from zero variance columns
      out[k] <- mean(abs(corrs), na.rm = TRUE)
      if (is.nan(out[k])) out[k] <- 0 # If all pairs had zero variance, result is NaN -> 0
    }
    out
  }

  ## ---------- defaults for shuffle / fit / measure ----------
  if (is.null(shuffle_fun))
    # Use the standardized helper name
    shuffle_fun <- .default_multiblock_shuffle
    # Original shuffle_fun <- function(Blocks, ...) lapply(Blocks, function(M) M[sample(N), , drop = FALSE])

  if (is.null(measure_fun))
    measure_fun <- default_measure

  ## ---------- observed statistic ----------
  Xp_obs <- prep_all(Xlist) # Preprocess original data
  Scores_obs <- get_block_scores(Xp_obs, Kmax) # Project using original model
  T_obs <- measure_fun(Scores_obs, Kmax, ...) # Measure consensus on observed scores

  ## ---------- permutation loop ----------
  worker <- function(iter, ...) {
    Xperm <- shuffle_fun(Xlist, ...) # Shuffle original data
    Xp    <- prep_all(Xperm)         # Preprocess shuffled data
    
    # Project shuffled preprocessed data using original model 'x'
    Scores_perm <- get_block_scores(Xp, Kmax) 
    
    # Measure consensus on permuted scores
    stat_perm <- measure_fun(Scores_perm, Kmax, ...)
    if (length(stat_perm) != Kmax) {
        warning(sprintf("Permutation %d: measure_fun returned %d values, expected %d. Filling with NA.", iter, length(stat_perm), Kmax))
        stat_perm_full <- rep(NA_real_, Kmax)
        valid_len <- min(length(stat_perm), Kmax)
        stat_perm_full[1:valid_len] <- stat_perm[1:valid_len]
        return(stat_perm_full)
    }
    stat_perm
  }

  applyFUN <- if (parallel) future.apply::future_lapply else lapply
  perm_mat <- do.call(rbind,
                      applyFUN(seq_len(nperm), worker, ...))
  ok       <- stats::complete.cases(perm_mat)
  if (!all(ok)) {
    warning(sum(!ok), " permutations failed; removed.")
    perm_mat <- perm_mat[ok, , drop = FALSE]
  }
  n_ok <- nrow(perm_mat)
  if (n_ok == 0) stop("All permutations failed.")

  ## ---------- p‑values & sequential stop ----------
  keep   <- seq_len(Kmax)
  pvals  <- rep(NA_real_, Kmax)
  stopat <- Kmax
  for (k in keep) {
    if (alternative == "greater")
      pvals[k] <- (sum(perm_mat[, k] >= T_obs[k]) + 1) / (n_ok + 1)
    else if (alternative == "less")
      pvals[k] <- (sum(perm_mat[, k] <= T_obs[k]) + 1) / (n_ok + 1)
    else {          # two‑sided
      p_high <- (sum(perm_mat[, k] >= T_obs[k]) + 1) / (n_ok + 1)
      p_low  <- (sum(perm_mat[, k] <= T_obs[k]) + 1) / (n_ok + 1)
      pvals[k] <- 2 * min(p_high, p_low)
    }
    if (pvals[k] > alpha) { stopat <- k; break }
  }

  keep <- seq_len(stopat)

  ci <- t(apply(perm_mat[, keep, drop = FALSE], 2,
                stats::quantile, probs = c(.025, .975)))

  comp_res <- data.frame(comp      = keep,
                         observed  = T_obs[keep],
                         p.value   = pvals[keep],
                         lower_ci  = ci[, 1],
                         upper_ci  = ci[, 2])

  structure(list(call              = match.call(),
                 component_results = comp_res,
                 perm_values       = perm_mat[, keep, drop = FALSE],
                 method            = "Permutation test for multiblock_projector (score-based consensus)",
                 nperm             = n_ok),
            class = c("perm_test_multiblock", "perm_test")
  )
}

1→#' construct a new pre-processing pipeline
     2→#' 
     3→#' Creates a bare prepper object (a pipeline holder).
     4→#' 
     5→#' TODO: Consider using a single environment in the finalized pre_processor
     6→#'       to store all step parameters, instead of individual environments
     7→#'       per step, potentially improving serialization and GC performance
     8→#'       for very large pipelines.
     9→#' 
    10→#' @keywords internal
    11→#' @noRd
    12→prepper <- function() {
    13→  steps <- list()
    14→  ret <- list(steps=steps)
    15→  class(ret) <- c("prepper", "list")
    16→  ret
    17→}
    18→
    19→#' Add a pre-processing node to a pipeline
    20→#' 
    21→#' @param x A `prepper` pipeline
    22→#' @param step The pre-processing step to add
    23→#' @param ... Additional arguments
    24→#' @export
    25→add_node.prepper <- function(x, step,...) {
    26→  x$steps[[length(x$steps)+1]] <- step
    27→  invisible(x) # Return invisibly for better pipe behavior
    28→}
    29→
    30→
    31→
    32→#' @export
    33→prep.prepper <- function(x,...) {
    34→  lifecycle::deprecate_warn(
    35→    "0.3.0", 
    36→    "prep()", 
    37→    "fit()",
    38→    details = "The prep() function is deprecated. Use fit() for a more standard interface."
    39→  )
    40→  
    41→  # Use local to capture a stable copy of steps
    42→  local({
    43→    steps <- x$steps
    44→    orig_ncol <- NULL # Placeholder for original number of columns
    45→    
    46→    # init transform: applies all forward steps and learns parameters
    47→    tinit <- function(X) {
    48→      xin <- X
    49→      # Store original dimension when initializing
    50→      assign("orig_ncol", ncol(X), envir = parent.env(environment()))
    51→      for (st in steps) {
    52→        xin <- st$forward(xin)
    53→      }
    54→      xin
    55→    }
    56→    
    57→    # transform: apply learned steps in forward direction using partial 'colind'
    58→    tform <- function(X, colind=NULL) {
    59→      xin <- X
    60→      for (st in steps) {
    61→        xin <- st$apply(xin, colind)
    62→      }
    63→      xin
    64→    }
    65→    
    66→    # reverse_transform: apply learned steps in reverse order
    67→    rtform <- function(X, colind=NULL) {
    68→      xin <- X
    69→      # Use rev() for clarity and robustness
    70→      for (st in rev(steps)) {
    71→        xin <- st$reverse(xin, colind)
    72→      }
    73→      xin
    74→    }
    75→    
    76→    ret <- list(
    77→      preproc = x, # Store the original prepper structure if needed
    78→      init = tinit,
    79→      transform = tform,
    80→      reverse_transform = rtform,
    81→      # Store orig_ncol after init is called
    82→      get_orig_ncol = function() orig_ncol 
    83→    )
    84→    
    85→    class(ret) <- "pre_processor"
    86→    ret
    87→  })
    88→}
    89→
    90→
    91→#' @export
    92→fresh.prepper <- function(x,...) {
    93→  p <- prepper()
    94→  for (step in x$steps) {
    95→    # Recreate each node by calling 'prep_node' again
    96→    p <- prep_node(p, step$name, step$create)
    97→  }
    98→  p
    99→}
   100→
   101→#' @export
   102→init_transform.pre_processor <- function(x, X,...) {
   103→  lifecycle::deprecate_warn(
   104→    "0.3.0", 
   105→    "init_transform()", 
   106→    "fit_transform()",
   107→    details = "init_transform() is deprecated. Use fit_transform() for a more standard interface."
   108→  )
   109→  
   110→  chk::chk_matrix(X)
   111→  res <- x$init(X)
   112→  # After init, orig_ncol should be set, store it directly in the object? 
   113→  # Modifying the object after creation might be tricky with environments.
   114→  # For now, rely on the closure environment within prep.prepper and get_orig_ncol.
   115→  res
   116→}
   117→
   118→#' @export
   119→apply_transform.pre_processor <- function(x, X, colind=NULL,...) {
   120→  lifecycle::deprecate_warn(
   121→    "0.3.0", 
   122→    "apply_transform()", 
   123→    "transform()",
   124→    details = "apply_transform() is deprecated. Use transform() for a more standard interface."
   125→  )
   126→  
   127→  chk::chk_matrix(X)
   128→  
   129→  # GENERAL CHECK: Ensure preprocessor is initialized before any application
   130→  # REMOVED: This check prevents using preprocessors with manually supplied parameters
   131→  # orig_ncol <- x$get_orig_ncol()
   132→  # if (is.null(orig_ncol)) {
   133→  #     stop("Preprocessor must be initialized with 'init_transform' before applying transformations.")
   134→  # }
   135→  
   136→  # COLIND SPECIFIC CHECKS: Only if colind is provided
   137→  if (!is.null(colind)) {
   138→    # Check colind against original dimension (orig_ncol is known to be non-NULL here)
   139→    # Need to get orig_ncol without stopping if NULL
   140→    orig_ncol <- x$get_orig_ncol() # May be NULL, that's okay now
   141→    chk::chk_vector(colind)
   142→    if (!is.null(orig_ncol)) { # Only check subset if orig_ncol is known
   143→        chk::chk_subset(colind, 1:orig_ncol)
   144→    }
   145→    # Ensure the provided X matches the dimension implied by colind
   146→    chk::chk_equal(ncol(X), length(colind))
   147→  }
   148→  
   149→  x$transform(X, colind)
   150→}
   151→
   152→#' @export
   153→reverse_transform.pre_processor <- function(x, X, colind=NULL,...) {
   154→  lifecycle::deprecate_warn(
   155→    "0.3.0", 
   156→    "reverse_transform()", 
   157→    "inverse_transform()",
   158→    details = "reverse_transform() is deprecated. Use inverse_transform() for a more standard interface."
   159→  )
   160→  
   161→  chk::chk_matrix(X)
   162→  
   163→  # GENERAL CHECK: Ensure preprocessor is initialized before any reversal
   164→  # REMOVED: This check prevents using preprocessors with manually supplied parameters
   165→  # orig_ncol <- x$get_orig_ncol()
   166→  # if (is.null(orig_ncol)) {
   167→  #     stop("Preprocessor must be initialized with 'init_transform' before reversing transformations.")
   168→  # }
   169→  
   170→  # COLIND SPECIFIC CHECKS: Only if colind is provided
   171→  if (!is.null(colind)) {
   172→    # Check colind against original dimension (orig_ncol is known to be non-NULL here)
   173→    # Need to get orig_ncol without stopping if NULL
   174→    orig_ncol <- x$get_orig_ncol() # May be NULL, that's okay now
   175→    chk::chk_vector(colind)
   176→     if (!is.null(orig_ncol)) { # Only check subset if orig_ncol is known
   177→        chk::chk_subset(colind, 1:orig_ncol)
   178→    }
   179→    # Ensure the provided X matches the dimension implied by colind
   180→    chk::chk_equal(ncol(X), length(colind))
   181→  }
   182→  
   183→  x$reverse_transform(X, colind)
   184→}
   185→
   186→#' @export
   187→fresh.pre_processor <- function(x, preproc=prepper(),...) {
   188→  # Attempt to recreate the original pipeline from stored steps in x$preproc
   189→  # similar to fresh.prepper
   190→  chk::chk_s3_class(x$preproc, "prepper")
   191→  fresh(x$preproc)
   192→}
   193→
   194→#' prepare a new node and add to pipeline
   195→#' 
   196→#' @param pipeline the pre-processing pipeline
   197→#' @param name the name of the step to add
   198→#' @param create the creation function
   199→#' 
   200→#' @keywords internal
   201→#' @noRd
   202→prep_node <- function(pipeline, name, create,  ...) {
   203→  node <- create()
   204→  ret <- list(name=name,
   205→              create=create,
   206→              forward=node$forward,
   207→              reverse=node$reverse,
   208→              apply=node$apply,
   209→              ...)
   210→  class(ret) <- c(name, "pre_processor")
   211→  add_node(pipeline, ret)
   212→}
   213→
   214→new_pre_processor <- function(x) {
   215→  chk::chk_not_null(x[["forward"]])
   216→  chk::chk_not_null(x[["apply"]])
   217→  chk::chk_not_null(x[["reverse"]])
   218→  chk::chk_function(x[["forward"]])
   219→  chk::chk_function(x[["apply"]])
   220→  chk::chk_function(x[["reverse"]])
   221→  
   222→  funlist <- x
   223→  structure(funlist,
   224→            class="pre_processing_step")
   225→}
   226→
   227→
   228→#' a no-op pre-processing step
   229→#' 
   230→#' `pass` simply passes its data through the chain
   231→#' 
   232→#' @param preproc the pre-processing pipeline
   233→#' @return a `prepper` list 
   234→#' @export
   235→pass <- function(preproc=prepper()) {
   236→  
   237→  create <- function() {
   238→    list(
   239→      forward = function(X, colind=NULL) {
   240→        X
   241→      },
   242→      
   243→      reverse = function(X, colind=NULL) {
   244→        X
   245→      },
   246→      
   247→      apply = function(X, colind=NULL) {
   248→        X
   249→      }
   250→    )
   251→  }
   252→  
   253→  prep_node(preproc, "pass", create)
   254→}
   255→
   256→
   257→#' center a data matrix
   258→#' 
   259→#' remove mean of all columns in matrix
   260→#' 
   261→#' @param cmeans optional vector of precomputed column means
   262→#' 
   263→#' @inheritParams pass
   264→#' @export
   265→#' @importFrom Matrix colMeans
   266→#' @return a `prepper` list 
   267→center <- function(preproc = prepper(), cmeans=NULL) {
   268→  create <- function() {
   269→    env <- rlang::new_environment()
   270→    env[["cmeans"]] <- cmeans
   271→    
   272→    list(
   273→      forward = function(X) {
   274→        if (is.null(env$cmeans)) {
   275→          cm <- colMeans(X)
   276→          env$cmeans <- cm
   277→        } else {
   278→          cm <- env$cmeans
   279→          chk::chk_equal(ncol(X), length(cm))
   280→        }
   281→        sweep(X, 2, cm, "-")
   282→      },
   283→      
   284→      apply = function(X, colind = NULL) {
   285→        cm <- env$cmeans
   286→        chk::chk_not_null(cm, "Means not initialized. Run init_transform first or supply 'cmeans'.")
   287→        if (is.null(colind)) {
   288→          sweep(X, 2, cm, "-")
   289→        } else {
   290→          chk::chk_equal(ncol(X), length(colind))
   291→          sweep(X, 2, cm[colind], "-")
   292→        }
   293→      },
   294→      
   295→      reverse = function(X, colind = NULL) {
   296→        chk::chk_not_null(env$cmeans)
   297→        if (is.null(colind)) {
   298→          # Use only the means corresponding to the columns present in X
   299→          nc <- ncol(X)
   300→          if (nc > length(env$cmeans)) {
   301→             stop(sprintf("Internal error in center$reverse: ncol(X) [%d] > length(stored means) [%d]", 
   302→                           nc, length(env$cmeans)))
   303→          } 
   304→          means_to_use <- env$cmeans[1:nc]
   305→          sweep(X, 2, means_to_use, "+")
   306→        } else {
   307→          chk::chk_equal(ncol(X), length(colind))
   308→          sweep(X, 2, env$cmeans[colind], "+")
   309→        }
   310→      }
   311→    )
   312→  }
   313→  
   314→  prep_node(preproc, "center", create)
   315→}
   316→
   317→
   318→#' scale a data matrix
   319→#' 
   320→#' normalize each column by a scale factor.
   321→#' 
   322→#' @inheritParams pass
   323→#' 
   324→#' @param type the kind of scaling, `unit` norm, `z`-scoring, or precomputed `weights`
   325→#' @param weights optional precomputed weights
   326→#' @return a `prepper` list 
   327→#' @export
   328→colscale <- function(preproc = prepper(),
   329→                     type = c("unit", "z", "weights"),
   330→                     weights = NULL) {
   331→  type <- match.arg(type)
   332→  
   333→  if (type != "weights" && !is.null(weights)) {
   334→    warning("colscale: weights ignored because type != 'weights'")
   335→  }
   336→  if (type == "weights") {
   337→    chk::chk_not_null(weights)
   338→  }
   339→  
   340→  create <- function() {
   341→    env <- rlang::new_environment()
   342→    env$weights <- weights # Store precomputed weights if provided
   343→    
   344→    list(
   345→      forward = function(X) {
   346→        if (is.null(env$weights)) { # Compute weights only if not precomputed
   347→          sds <- matrixStats::colSds(as.matrix(X)) # Ensure matrix for colSds
   348→          
   349→          # Handle zero standard deviations robustly
   350→          zero_sd <- sds < .Machine$double.eps
   351→          if (any(zero_sd)) {
   352→              warning(sprintf("Columns %s have zero standard deviation. Setting scale factor to 1.", 
   353→                              paste(which(zero_sd), collapse=", ")))
   354→          }
   355→          sds[zero_sd] <- 1 # Set zero SDs to 1 to avoid Inf weights
   356→          
   357→          if (type == "unit") {
   358→            # Unit norm scaling: weight is 1 / (sd * sqrt(N-1))
   359→            # Ensure N > 1 for sqrt
   360→            N <- nrow(X)
   361→            if (N <= 1) stop("Cannot compute unit norm scaling with N <= 1.")
   362→            scaling_factor <- sds * sqrt(N - 1)
   363→            # Check for zeros again after scaling (unlikely but possible)
   364→            scaling_factor[scaling_factor < .Machine$double.eps] <- 1
   365→            wts <- 1 / scaling_factor
   366→          } else { # type == "z"
   367→            # z-scaling weight is 1 / sd
   368→            wts <- 1 / sds
   369→          }
   370→          env$weights <- wts
   371→        } else {
   372→            wts <- env$weights
   373→            chk::chk_equal(length(wts), ncol(X))
   374→        }
   375→        sweep(X, 2, wts, "*")
   376→      },
   377→      
   378→      apply = function(X, colind = NULL) {
   379→        chk::chk_not_null(env$weights, "Weights not initialized. Run init_transform first.")
   380→        wts <- env$weights
   381→        if (is.null(colind)) {
   382→          sweep(X, 2, wts, "*")
   383→        } else {
   384→          # Ensure X matches colind length, and colind is valid (checked by caller)
   385→          chk::chk_equal(ncol(X), length(colind))
   386→          sweep(X, 2, wts[colind], "*")
   387→        }
   388→      },
   389→      
   390→      reverse = function(X, colind = NULL) {
   391→        chk::chk_not_null(env$weights, "Weights not initialized. Run init_transform first.")
   392→        wts <- env$weights
   393→        # Handle potential division by zero if weights were somehow zero
   394→        wts_safe <- wts
   395→        wts_safe[abs(wts) < .Machine$double.eps] <- 1 # Avoid division by zero
   396→        
   397→        if (is.null(colind)) {
   398→          # Use only the weights corresponding to the columns present in X
   399→          nc <- ncol(X)
   400→           if (nc > length(wts_safe)) {
   401→             stop(sprintf("Internal error in colscale$reverse: ncol(X) [%d] > length(stored weights) [%d]", 
   402→                           nc, length(wts_safe)))
   403→           } 
   404→          weights_to_use <- wts_safe[1:nc]
   405→          sweep(X, 2, weights_to_use, "/")
   406→        } else {
   407→          chk::chk_equal(ncol(X), length(colind))
   408→          sweep(X, 2, wts_safe[colind], "/")
   409→        }
   410→      }
   411→    )
   412→  }
   413→  
   414→  prep_node(preproc, "colscale", create)
   415→}
   416→
   417→
   418→#' center and scale each vector of a matrix
   419→#' 
   420→#' @param cmeans an optional vector of column means
   421→#' @param sds an optional vector of sds
   422→#' @inheritParams pass
   423→#' @return a `prepper` list 
   424→#' @export
   425→standardize <- function(preproc = prepper(), cmeans=NULL, sds=NULL) {
   426→  create <- function() {
   427→    env <- rlang::new_environment()
   428→    list(
   429→      forward = function(X) {
   430→        if (is.null(sds)) {
   431→          sds2 <- matrixStats::colSds(X)
   432→        } else {
   433→          chk::chk_equal(length(sds), ncol(X))
   434→          sds2 <- sds
   435→        }
   436→        
   437→        if (is.null(cmeans)) {
   438→          cmeans2 <- colMeans(X)
   439→        } else {
   440→          chk::chk_equal(length(cmeans), ncol(X))
   441→          cmeans2 <- cmeans
   442→        }
   443→        
   444→        if (all(sds2 == 0)) {
   445→          sds2[] <- mean(sds2[sds2>0], na.rm=TRUE)
   446→          sds2[is.na(sds2)] <- 1 # fallback if all zero
   447→        }
   448→        
   449→        env$sds <- sds2
   450→        env$cmeans <- cmeans2
   451→        
   452→        # TODO: [INEFF] This uses two sweep() calls. For large matrices, 
   453→        #       consider combining into a single pass or using optimized 
   454→        #       functions if performance becomes critical.
   455→        x1 <- sweep(X, 2, cmeans2, "-")
   456→        sweep(x1, 2, sds2, "/")
   457→      },
   458→      
   459→      apply = function(X, colind = NULL) {
   460→        sds2 <- env$sds
   461→        cmeans2 <- env$cmeans
   462→        chk::chk_not_null(sds2, "SDs not initialized. Run init_transform first or supply 'sds'.")
   463→        chk::chk_not_null(cmeans2, "Means not initialized. Run init_transform first or supply 'cmeans'.")
   464→        if (is.null(colind)) {
   465→          # TODO: [INEFF] See forward() - two sweep() calls.
   466→          x1 <- sweep(X, 2, cmeans2, "-")
   467→          sweep(x1, 2, sds2, "/")
   468→        } else {
   469→          chk::chk_equal(ncol(X), length(colind))
   470→          # TODO: [INEFF] See forward() - two sweep() calls.
   471→          x1 <- sweep(X, 2, cmeans2[colind], "-")
   472→          sweep(x1, 2, sds2[colind], "/")
   473→        }
   474→      },
   475→      
   476→      reverse = function(X, colind = NULL) {
   477→        sds2 <- env$sds
   478→        cmeans2 <- env$cmeans
   479→        chk::chk_not_null(sds2, "SDs not initialized.")
   480→        chk::chk_not_null(cmeans2, "Means not initialized.")
   481→        
   482→        if (is.null(colind)) {
   483→          # Use only parameters corresponding to columns in X
   484→          nc <- ncol(X)
   485→          if (nc > length(sds2) || nc > length(cmeans2)) {
   486→               stop(sprintf("Internal error in standardize$reverse: ncol(X) [%d] inconsistent with stored params [%d, %d]", 
   487→                           nc, length(sds2), length(cmeans2)))
   488→          }
   489→          sds_to_use <- sds2[1:nc]
   490→          means_to_use <- cmeans2[1:nc]
   491→          
   492→          # TODO: [INEFF] Two sweep() calls.
   493→          x0 <- sweep(X, 2, sds_to_use, "*")
   494→          sweep(x0, 2, means_to_use, "+")
   495→        } else {
   496→          chk::chk_equal(ncol(X), length(colind))
   497→          # TODO: [INEFF] Two sweep() calls.
   498→          x0 <- sweep(X, 2, sds2[colind], "*")
   499→          sweep(x0, 2, cmeans2[colind], "+")
   500→        }
   501→      }
   502→    )
   503→  }
   504→  prep_node(preproc, "standardize", create)
   505→}
   506→
   507→
   508→#' bind together blockwise pre-processors
   509→#' 
   510→#' concatenate a sequence of pre-processors, each applied to a block of data.
   511→#' 
   512→#' @param preprocs a list of initialized `pre_processor` objects
   513→#' @param block_indices a list of integer vectors specifying the global column indices for each block
   514→#' @return a new `pre_processor` object that applies the correct transformations blockwise
   515→#' @examples 
   516→#' 
   517→#' p1 <- center() |> prep()
   518→#' p2 <- center() |> prep()
   519→#' 
   520→#' x1 <- rbind(1:10, 2:11)
   521→#' x2 <- rbind(1:10, 2:11)
   522→#' 
   523→#' p1a <- init_transform(p1,x1)
   524→#' p2a <- init_transform(p2,x2)
   525→#' 
   526→#' clist <- concat_pre_processors(list(p1,p2), list(1:10, 11:20))
   527→#' t1 <- apply_transform(clist, cbind(x1,x2))
   528→#' 
   529→#' t2 <- apply_transform(clist, cbind(x1,x2[,1:5]), colind=1:15)
   530→#' @export
   531→concat_pre_processors <- function(preprocs, block_indices) {
   532→  chk::chk_equal(length(preprocs), length(block_indices))
   533→  
   534→  unraveled_ids <- unlist(block_indices)
   535→  # Check for overlaps and completeness more thoroughly?
   536→  if (any(duplicated(unraveled_ids))) stop("Duplicate indices found in block_indices.")
   537→  # Assuming indices cover a contiguous range for simplicity now, but could add checks.
   538→  max_idx <- max(unraveled_ids)
   539→  
   540→  # Precompute mapping for efficiency and correct ordering
   541→  map_list <- lapply(seq_along(block_indices), function(i) {
   542→      list(orig_indices = block_indices[[i]], 
   543→           proc = preprocs[[i]])
   544→  })
   545→  names(map_list) <- as.character(seq_along(map_list))
   546→
   547→  # Internal helper for applying functions blockwise respecting colind order
   548→  # TODO: [INEFF] This helper recalculates mappings on each call.
   549→  #       If called repeatedly with the same colind structure (unlikely?),
   550→  #       pre-calculating and caching the exact mappings might be faster.
   551→  apply_blockwise <- function(func, X, colind) {
   552→    chk::chk_matrix(X)
   553→    chk::chk_vector(colind)
   554→    # Remove unsupported named arguments x_arg, y_arg
   555→    chk::chk_equal(ncol(X), length(colind))
   556→    
   557→    results_list <- vector("list", length(colind))
   558→    processed_cols <- logical(length(colind)) # Track which columns are processed
   559→    
   560→    # Find which original blocks are relevant for the given colind
   561→    for (block_num in seq_along(map_list)) {
   562→        block_info <- map_list[[block_num]]
   563→        # Find which of the requested `colind` fall into this block's original indices
   564→        matched_in_colind_idx <- which(colind %in% block_info$orig_indices)
   565→        
   566→        if (length(matched_in_colind_idx) > 0) {
   567→            # Get the subset of X corresponding to these columns
   568→            X_subset <- X[, matched_in_colind_idx, drop = FALSE]
   569→            # Get the original GLOBAL indices within this block that correspond to X_subset
   570→            orig_indices_in_block <- colind[matched_in_colind_idx]
   571→            
   572→            # Map global indices to the LOCAL indices expected by the block's preprocessor
   573→            local_indices_for_proc <- match(orig_indices_in_block, block_info$orig_indices)
   574→            if (any(is.na(local_indices_for_proc))) {
   575→                stop("Internal error: Mismatch between requested global indices and block's original indices.")
   576→            }
   577→            
   578→            # Apply the function using the LOCAL indices relative to the block
   579→            res_subset <- func(block_info$proc, X_subset, colind = local_indices_for_proc)
   580→            
   581→            # Place results back in the correct positions in the output list
   582→            results_list[matched_in_colind_idx] <- lapply(seq_len(ncol(res_subset)), function(k) res_subset[,k])
   583→            processed_cols[matched_in_colind_idx] <- TRUE
   584→        }
   585→    }
   586→    
   587→    if (!all(processed_cols)) {
   588→        stop("Some requested columns in 'colind' did not map to any provided block.")
   589→    }
   590→    
   591→    # Combine results respecting original colind order
   592→    do.call(cbind, results_list)
   593→  }
   594→  
   595→  ret <- list(
   596→    # Note: init_transform is not defined for concat_pre_processor
   597→    # User should initialize individual preprocs before concatenating.
   598→    transform = function(X, colind = NULL) {
   599→      chk::chk_matrix(X)
   600→      if (is.null(colind)) {
   601→        # Apply to full blocks in their natural order
   602→        chk::chk_equal(ncol(X), length(unraveled_ids))
   603→        res_list <- lapply(seq_along(map_list), function(i) {
   604→          block_info <- map_list[[i]]
   605→          apply_transform(block_info$proc, X[, block_info$orig_indices, drop = FALSE])
   606→        })
   607→        do.call(cbind, res_list)
   608→      } else {
   609→        # Apply blockwise respecting colind order
   610→        apply_blockwise(apply_transform, X, colind)
   611→      }
   612→    },
   613→    reverse_transform = function(X, colind = NULL) {
   614→      chk::chk_matrix(X)
   615→      if (is.null(colind)) {
   616→        # Reverse full blocks in their natural order
   617→        chk::chk_equal(ncol(X), length(unraveled_ids))
   618→        res_list <- lapply(seq_along(map_list), function(i) {
   619→          block_info <- map_list[[i]]
   620→          reverse_transform(block_info$proc, X[, block_info$orig_indices, drop = FALSE])
   621→        })
   622→        do.call(cbind, res_list)
   623→      } else {
   624→        # Reverse blockwise respecting colind order
   625→        apply_blockwise(reverse_transform, X, colind)
   626→      }
   627→    }
   628→    # Store map_list? Might be useful for introspection.
   629→    # map_list = map_list 
   630→  )
   631→  
   632→  class(ret) <- c("concat_pre_processor", "pre_processor")
   633→  ret
   634→}
   635→
   636→
   637→
   638→#' @export
   639→apply_transform.concat_pre_processor <- function(x, X, colind=NULL,...) {
   640→  # Directly call the transform function stored within the concat object
   641→  x$transform(X, colind, ...)
   642→}
   643→
   644→#' @export
   645→reverse_transform.concat_pre_processor <- function(x, X, colind=NULL,...) {
   646→  # Directly call the reverse_transform function stored within the concat object
   647→  x$reverse_transform(X, colind, ...)
   648→}
   649→
   650→#' Print a prepper pipeline
   651→#' 
   652→#' Uses `crayon` to produce a colorful and readable representation of the pipeline steps.
   653→#'
   654→#' @param x A `prepper` object.
   655→#' @param ... Additional arguments (ignored).
   656→#' @export
   657→print.prepper <- function(x,...) {
   658→  nn <- sapply(x$steps, function(st) st$name)
   659→  if (length(nn) == 0) {
   660→    cat(crayon::cyan("A preprocessor with no steps.\n"))
   661→    return(invisible(x))
   662→  }
   663→  
   664→  cat(crayon::bold(crayon::green("Preprocessor pipeline:\n")))
   665→  for (i in seq_along(nn)) {
   666→    cat(crayon::magenta(" Step ", i, ": "), crayon::cyan(nn[i]), "\n", sep="")
   667→  }
   668→  invisible(x)
   669→}
   670→
   671→
   672→#' Print a pre_processor object
   673→#' 
   674→#' Display information about a `pre_processor` using crayon-based formatting.
   675→#' 
   676→#' @param x A `pre_processor` object.
   677→#' @param ... Additional arguments (ignored).
   678→#' @export
   679→print.pre_processor <- function(x, ...) {
   680→  # A pre_processor comes from prep(prepper)
   681→  # It has x$preproc to show original steps.
   682→  # Let's show the chain of steps and indicate it's finalized.
   683→  
   684→  cat(crayon::bold(crayon::green("A finalized pre-processing pipeline:\n")))
   685→  if (!is.null(x$preproc) && inherits(x$preproc, "prepper")) {
   686→    nn <- sapply(x$preproc$steps, function(st) st$name)
   687→    if (length(nn) == 0) {
   688→      cat(crayon::cyan("  No steps.\n"))
   689→    } else {
   690→      for (i in seq_along(nn)) {
   691→        cat(crayon::magenta(" Step ", i, ": "), crayon::cyan(nn[i]), "\n", sep="")
   692→      }
   693→    }
   694→  } else {
   695→    cat(crayon::cyan("  No associated prepper information.\n"))
   696→  }
   697→  invisible(x)
   698→}
   699→
   700→
   701→#' Print a concat_pre_processor object
   702→#' 
   703→#' @param x A `concat_pre_processor` object.
   704→#' @param ... Additional arguments (ignored).
   705→#' @export
   706→print.concat_pre_processor <- function(x, ...) {
   707→  cat(crayon::bold(crayon::green("A concatenated (blockwise) pre-processing pipeline:\n")))
   708→  cat(crayon::cyan("  This object applies different pre-processors to distinct column blocks.\n"))
   709→  invisible(x)
   710→}
   711→
   712→
   713→# =============================================================================
   714→# New S3 Methods for Modern Preprocessing API
   715→# =============================================================================
   716→
   717→#' @export
   718→fit.prepper <- function(object, X, ...) {
   719→  proc <- prep(object)
   720→  fitted_proc <- mark_fitted(proc, TRUE)
   721→  init_transform(fitted_proc, X)
   722→  fitted_proc
   723→}
   724→
   725→#' @export
   726→fit_transform.prepper <- function(object, X, ...) {
   727→  proc <- prep(object)
   728→  fitted_proc <- mark_fitted(proc, TRUE)
   729→  transformed <- init_transform(fitted_proc, X)
   730→  list(preproc = fitted_proc, transformed = transformed)
   731→}
   732→
   733→#' @export
   734→transform.pre_processor <- function(object, X, ...) {
   735→  check_fitted(object, "transform")
   736→  apply_transform(object, X, ...)
   737→}
   738→
   739→#' @export
   740→inverse_transform.pre_processor <- function(object, X, ...) {
   741→  check_fitted(object, "inverse_transform")
   742→  reverse_transform(object, X, ...)
   743→}
   744→
   745→#' @export
   746→transform.concat_pre_processor <- function(object, X, ...) {
   747→  check_fitted(object, "transform")
   748→  apply_transform(object, X, ...)
   749→}
   750→
   751→#' @export
   752→inverse_transform.concat_pre_processor <- function(object, X, ...) {
   753→  check_fitted(object, "inverse_transform")
   754→  reverse_transform(object, X, ...)
   755→}

1→#' New sample projection
     2→#'
     3→#' Project one or more samples onto a subspace. This function takes a model fit and new observations, and projects them onto the
     4→#' subspace defined by the model. This allows for the transformation of new data into the same lower-dimensional space as the original data.
     5→#'
     6→#' @param x The model fit, typically an object of class bi_projector or any other class that implements a project method
     7→#' @param new_data A matrix or vector of new observations with the same number of columns as the original data. Rows represent observations and columns represent variables
     8→#' @param ... Extra arguments to be passed to the specific project method for the object's class
     9→#' @return A matrix or vector of the projected observations, where rows represent observations and columns represent the lower-dimensional space
    10→#' @export
    11→#' @family project
    12→#' @seealso \code{\link{bi_projector}} for an example of a class that implements a project method
    13→#' @examples
    14→#' # Example with the bi_projector class
    15→#' X <- matrix(rnorm(10*20), 10, 20)
    16→#' svdfit <- svd(X)
    17→#' p <- bi_projector(svdfit$v, s = svdfit$u %*% diag(svdfit$d), sdev=svdfit$d)
    18→#'
    19→#' # Project new_data onto the same subspace as the original data
    20→#' new_data <- matrix(rnorm(5*20), 5, 20)
    21→#' projected_data <- project(p, new_data)
    22→project <- function(x, new_data, ...) UseMethod("project")
    23→
    24→
    25→
    26→#' Partially project a new sample onto subspace
    27→#'
    28→#' Project a selected subset of column indices (`colind`) of `new_data` onto
    29→#' the subspace defined by the model \code{x}. Optionally do a
    30→#' ridge-regularized least-squares solve if columns are non-orthonormal.
    31→#'
    32→#' @param x The fitted model, e.g. `bi_projector`, that has a partial_project method.
    33→#' @param new_data A numeric matrix (n x length(colind)) or vector, representing
    34→#'   the observations to be projected.
    35→#' @param colind A numeric vector of column indices in the original data space
    36→#'   that correspond to \code{new_data}'s columns.
    37→#' @param least_squares Logical; if TRUE (default), do a ridge-regularized solve.
    38→#' @param lambda Numeric; ridge penalty (default 1e-6). Ignored if `least_squares=FALSE`.
    39→#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to class-specific partial_project methods.
    40→#'
    41→#' @return A numeric matrix (n x d) of factor scores in the model's subspace, for
    42→#'   those columns only.
    43→#' @export
    44→partial_project <- function(x, new_data, colind,
    45→                            least_squares = TRUE,
    46→                            lambda = 1e-6,
    47→                            ...) {
    48→  UseMethod("partial_project")
    49→}

    54→#' Construct a partial projector
    55→#'
    56→#' Create a new projector instance restricted to a subset of input columns. This function allows for the generation of
    57→#' a new projection object that focuses only on the specified columns, enabling the projection of data using a limited
    58→#' set of variables.
    59→#'
    60→#' @param x The original `projector` instance, typically an object of class `bi_projector` or any other class that implements a `partial_projector` method
    61→#' @param colind A numeric vector of column indices to select in the projection matrix. These indices correspond to the variables used for the partial projector
    62→#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to the underlying `partial_projector` method
    63→#' @return A new `projector` instance, with the same class as the original object, that is restricted to the specified subset of input columns
    64→#' @export
    65→#' @seealso \code{\link{bi_projector}} for an example of a class that implements a `partial_projector` method
    66→#' @examples
    67→#' # Example with the bi_projector class
    68→#' X <- matrix(rnorm(10*20), 10, 20)
    69→#' svdfit <- svd(X)
    70→#' p <- bi_projector(svdfit$v, s = svdfit$u %*% diag(svdfit$d), sdev=svdfit$d)
    71→#'
    72→#' # Create a partial projector using only the first 10 variables
    73→#' colind <- 1:10
    74→#' partial_p <- partial_projector(p, colind)
    75→partial_projector <- function(x, colind, ...) UseMethod("partial_projector")

    86→#' @param x The model fit, typically an object of a class that implements a `project_block` method

    89→#' @param least_squares Logical. If `TRUE` use least squares projection.
    90→#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to the underlying `project_block` method
    91→#' @return A matrix or vector of the projected data for the specified block
    92→#' @export
    93→#' @family project
    94→#' @seealso \code{\link{project}} for the generic projection function
    95→project_block <- function(x, new_data, block, least_squares, ...) UseMethod("project_block")

   103→#' projection and can be computed for a biorthogonal decomposition, such as Singular Value Decomposition (SVD).

   115→#' Transpose a model
   116→#'
   117→#' This function transposes a model by switching coefficients and scores. It is useful when you want to
   118→#' reverse the roles of samples and variables in a model, especially in the context of dimensionality
   119→#' reduction methods.
   120→#'
   121→#' @param x The model fit, typically an object of a class that implements a `transpose` method

   124→#' @export

   131→#' Reconstruct the data
   132→#'
   133→#' Reconstruct a data set from its (possibly) low-rank representation. This can be useful when analyzing
   134→#' the impact of dimensionality reduction or when visualizing approximations of the original data.
   135→#'
   136→#' @param x The model fit, typically an object of a class that implements a `reconstruct` method
   137→#' @param comp A vector of component indices to use in the reconstruction
   138→#' @param rowind The row indices to reconstruct (optional). If not provided, all rows are used.
   139→#' @param colind The column indices to reconstruct (optional). If not provided, all columns are used.
   140→#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to the underlying `reconstruct` method
   141→#' @return A reconstructed data set based on the selected components, rows, and columns
   142→#' @export
   143→#' @family reconstruct
   144→#' @seealso \code{\link{bi_projector}} for an example of a two-way mapping model that can be reconstructed
   145→reconstruct <- function(x, comp, rowind, colind, ...) UseMethod("reconstruct")

   149→#' Reconstruct new data in a model's subspace
   150→#'
   151→#' This function takes a model (e.g., `projector` or `bi_projector`) and a new dataset,
   152→#' and computes the rank-d approximation of the new data in the same subspace that
   153→#' was defined by the model. In other words, we \strong{project} the new data into
   154→#' the fitted subspace and then \strong{map it back} to the original dimensionality.
   155→#'
   156→#' Similar to \code{\link{reconstruct}} but operates on an external \code{new_data}
   157→#' rather than the original fitted data. Often used to see how well the model's
   158→#' subspace explains unseen data.
   159→#'
   160→#' @param x The fitted model object (e.g., \code{bi_projector}) that defines
   161→#'   a subspace or factorization.
   162→#' @param new_data A numeric matrix (or data frame) of shape
   163→#'   \code{(n x p_full)} or possibly fewer columns if you allow partial reconstruction.
   164→#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to the specific \code{reconstruct_new} method
   165→#'   for the class of \code{x}.
   166→#'
   167→#' @return A numeric matrix (same number of rows as \code{new_data}, and typically
   168→#'   the same number of columns if you're reconstructing fully) representing the
   169→#'   rank-d approximation in the model's subspace.
   170→#'
   171→#' @export
   172→#' @family reconstruct
   173→#' @seealso \code{\link{reconstruct}} for reconstructing the original data in the model.
   174→reconstruct_new <- function(x, new_data, ...) {
   175→  UseMethod("reconstruct_new")
   176→}

   180→#' Transfer data from one domain/block to another via a latent space
   181→#'
   182→#' Convert between data representations in a multiblock or cross-decomposition
   183→#' model by projecting the input `new_data` from the `from` domain/block
   184→#' onto a latent space and then reconstructing it in the `to` domain/block.
   185→#'
   186→#' @param x The model fit, typically an object that implements a `transfer` method
   187→#'   and ideally a `block_names` method.
   188→#' @param new_data The data to transfer, typically matching the dimension of the `from` domain.
   189→#' @param from Character string or index identifying the source domain/block.
   190→#'   Must be present in `block_names(x)` if that method exists.
   191→#' @param to Character string or index identifying the target domain/block.
   192→#'   Must be present in `block_names(x)` if that method exists.
   193→#' @param opts A list of optional arguments controlling the transfer process:
   194→#'   \describe{
   195→#'     \item{`cols`}{Optional numeric vector specifying column indices of the *target* 
   196→#'       domain to reconstruct. If NULL (default), reconstructs all columns.}
   197→#'     \item{`comps`}{Optional numeric vector specifying which latent components to use 
   198→#'       for the projection/reconstruction. If NULL (default), uses all components.}
   199→#'     \item{`ls_rr`}{Logical; if TRUE, use a ridge-regularized LS approach for the 
   200→#'       initial projection from the `from` domain. Default FALSE.}
   201→#'     \item{`lambda`}{Numeric ridge penalty (if `ls_rr=TRUE`). Default 1e-6.}
   202→#'   }
   203→#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to specific methods (discouraged, prefer `opts`).
   204→#'
   205→#' @return A matrix or data frame representing the transferred data in the `to` domain/block 
   206→#'   (or a subset of columns/components if specified in `opts`).
   207→#' @export
   208→transfer <- function(x, new_data, from, to, opts = list(), ...) {
   209→  UseMethod("transfer")
   210→}

   212→#' Default method for transfer

   218→transfer.default <- function(x, ...) {
   219→    cli::cli_abort(c(

   221→      "i" = "Provide a method or ensure the object inherits from a supported class."
   222→      # Consider adding: "i" = "Supported classes include: cross_projector, ..."
   223→    ))
   224→}

   591→
   592→#' Generic Permutation-Based Test
   593→#'
   594→#' This generic function implements a permutation-based test to assess the significance
   595→#' of components or statistics in a fitted model. The actual procedure depends on
   596→#' the method defined for the specific model class. Typical usage:
   597→#'
   598→#' \enumerate{
   599→#'   \item Shuffle or permute the data in a way that breaks the structure of interest
   600→#'         (e.g., shuffle labels for supervised methods, shuffle columns/rows for unsupervised).
   601→#'   \item Re-fit or re-project the model on the permuted data. Depending on the class,
   602→#'         this can be done via a \code{fit_fun} or a class-specific approach.
   603→#'   \item Measure the statistic of interest (e.g., variance explained, classification accuracy, canonical correlation).
   604→#'   \item Compare the distribution of permuted statistics to the observed statistic to compute an empirical p-value.
   605→#' }
   606→#'
   607→#' S3 methods define the specific defaults and required signatures for the functions
   608→#' involved in shuffling, fitting, and measuring.
   609→#'
   610→#' @name perm_test
   611→#' @aliases perm_test perm_test.pca perm_test.cross_projector perm_test.discriminant_projector perm_test.multiblock_biprojector
   612→#'
   613→#' @usage perm_test(x, ...)
   614→#'
   615→#' @param x A fitted model object (e.g. \code{pca}, \code{cross_projector}, \code{discriminant_projector}, \code{multiblock_biprojector}).
   616→#' @param X (Used by \code{pca}, \code{cross_projector}, \code{discriminant_projector}) The original primary data matrix used to fit \code{x}. Ignored by the \code{multiblock_biprojector} method.
   617→#' @param Y (Used by \code{cross_projector}) The secondary data block (n x pY). Ignored by other methods.
   618→#' @param Xlist (Used by \code{multiblock_biprojector} \[optional, default \code{NULL}\] and \code{multiblock_projector} \[required\]) List of data blocks.
   619→#' @param nperm Integer number of permutations (Default: 1000 for PCA, 500 for multiblock methods, 100 otherwise).
   620→#' @param measure_fun (Optional; Used by \code{pca}, \code{cross_projector}, \code{discriminant_projector}, \code{multiblock_projector}) A function for computing the statistic(s) of interest. Ignored by \code{multiblock_biprojector}. Signature/default varies by method (see Details).
   621→#' @param shuffle_fun (Optional; Used by all methods) A function for permuting the data appropriately. Signature/default varies by method (see Details).
   622→#' @param fit_fun (Optional; Used by \code{cross_projector}, \code{discriminant_projector}) A function for re-fitting a new model. Ignored by PCA and multiblock methods. Signature/default varies by method (see Details).
   623→#' @param stepwise (Used by \code{pca}) Logical indicating if sequential testing (P3 projection) should be performed. Default \code{TRUE}. (The multiblock methods also perform sequential testing based on \code{alpha} and \code{comps}, but this argument is ignored). Ignored by other methods.
   624→#' @param parallel (Used by all methods) Logical; if `TRUE`, attempt parallel execution via `future.apply::future_lapply`.
   625→#' @param alternative (Used by all methods) Character string for the alternative hypothesis: "greater" (default), "less", or "two.sided".
   626→#' @param alpha (Used by \code{pca}, \code{multiblock_biprojector}, \code{multiblock_projector}) Significance level for sequential stopping rule (default 0.05). Passed directly as a named argument to these methods.
   627→#' @param comps (Used by \code{pca}, \code{multiblock_biprojector}, \code{multiblock_projector}) Maximum number of components to test sequentially (default 4). Passed directly as a named argument to these methods.
   628→#' @param use_svd_solver (Used by \code{pca}) Optional string specifying the SVD solver (default "fast").
   629→#' @param use_rspectra (Used by \code{multiblock_biprojector}) Logical indicating whether to use RSpectra for eigenvalue calculation (default \code{TRUE}). Passed directly as a named argument.
   630→#' @param predict_method (Used by \code{discriminant_projector}) Prediction method (`"lda"` or `"euclid"`) used by the default measure function (default "lda").
   631→#' @param ... Additional arguments passed down to `shuffle_fun` or `measure_fun` (if applicable).
   632→#'   Note: For \code{multiblock} methods, \code{Xlist}, \code{comps}, \code{alpha}, and \code{use_rspectra} (for biprojector) are handled as direct named arguments, not via \code{...}.
   633→#'
   634→#' @details
   635→#' This function provides a framework for permutation testing in various multivariate models.
   636→#' The specific implementation details, default functions, and relevant arguments vary by method.
   637→#'
   638→#' \strong{PCA Method (`perm_test.pca`):} 
   639→#' Relevant arguments: \code{X}, \code{nperm}, \code{measure_fun}, \code{shuffle_fun}, \code{stepwise}, \code{parallel}, \code{alternative}, \code{alpha}, \code{comps}, \code{use_svd_solver}, \code{...}. Assesses significance of variance explained by each PC (Vitale et al., 2017). Default statistic: F_a. Default shuffle: column-wise. Default uses P3 projection and sequential stopping with \code{alpha}.
   640→#'
   641→#' \strong{Cross Projector Method (`perm_test.cross_projector`):} 
   642→#' Relevant arguments: \code{X}, \code{Y}, \code{nperm}, \code{measure_fun}, \code{shuffle_fun}, \code{fit_fun}, \code{parallel}, \code{alternative}, \code{...}. Tests the X-Y relationship. Default statistic: `x2y.mse`. Default shuffle: rows of Y. Default fit: `stats::cancor`.
   643→#'
   644→#' \strong{Discriminant Projector Method (`perm_test.discriminant_projector`):} 
   645→#' Relevant arguments: \code{X}, \code{nperm}, \code{measure_fun}, \code{shuffle_fun}, \code{fit_fun}, \code{predict_method}, \code{parallel}, \code{alternative}, \code{...}. Tests class separation. Default statistic: prediction accuracy. Default shuffle: labels. Default fit: `MASS::lda`.
   646→#'
   647→#' \strong{Multiblock Bi-Projector Method (`perm_test.multiblock_biprojector`):} 
   648→#' Relevant arguments: \code{Xlist} (optional), \code{nperm}, \code{shuffle_fun}, \code{parallel}, \code{alternative}, \code{alpha}, \code{comps}, \code{use_rspectra}, \code{...}. Tests consensus using fixed internal statistic (eigenvalue) on scores for each component. The statistic is the leading eigenvalue of the covariance matrix of block scores for a given component (T^T, where T columns are scores of block \emph{b} on component \emph{k}). By default, it shuffles rows within each block independently (either from \code{Xlist} if provided via \code{...}, or using the internally stored scores). It performs sequential testing for components specified by \code{comps} using the stopping rule defined by \code{alpha} (both passed via \code{...}).
   649→#'
   650→#' \strong{Multiblock Projector Method (`perm_test.multiblock_projector`):} 
   651→#' Relevant arguments: \code{Xlist} (required), \code{nperm}, \code{measure_fun}, \code{shuffle_fun}, \code{parallel}, \code{alternative}, \code{alpha}, \code{comps}, \code{...}. Tests consensus using \code{measure_fun} (default: mean abs corr) on scores projected from \code{Xlist} using the original model \code{x}. Does not refit.
   652→#'
   653→#' @return
   654→#' The structure of the return value depends on the method:
   655→#' \describe{
   656→#'   \item{\strong{`cross_projector`} and \strong{`discriminant_projector`}:}{
   657→#'     Returns an object of class \code{perm_test}, a list containing: \code{statistic}, \code{perm_values}, \code{p.value}, \code{alternative}, \code{method}, \code{nperm}, \code{call}.}
   658→#'   \item{\strong{`pca`}, \strong{`multiblock_biprojector`}, and \strong{`multiblock_projector`}:}{
   659→#'     Returns an object inheriting from \code{perm_test} (classes \code{perm_test_pca}, \code{perm_test_multiblock}, or \code{perm_test} respectively for multiblock_projector), 
   660→#'     a list containing: \code{component_results} (data frame with observed stat, pval, CIs per component), \code{perm_values} (matrix of permuted stats), \code{alpha} (if applicable), \code{alternative}, \code{method}, \code{nperm} (vector of successful permutations per component), \code{call}.}
   661→#' }
   662→#'
   663→#' @references
   664→#' Buja, A., & Eyuboglu, N. (1992). Remarks on parallel analysis. *Multivariate Behavioral Research*, 27(4), 509-540. (Relevant for PCA permutation concepts)
   665→#'
   666→#' Vitale, R., Westerhuis, J. A., Næs, T., Smilde, A. K., de Noord, O. E., & Ferrer, A. (2017).
   667→#' Selecting the number of factors in principal component analysis by permutation testing—
   668→#' Numerical and practical aspects. *Journal of Chemometrics*, 31(10), e2937.
   669→#' \doi{10.1002/cem.2937} (Specific to `perm_test.pca`)
   670→#'
   671→#' @seealso \code{\link{pca}}, \code{\link{cross_projector}}, \code{\link{discriminant_projector}},
   672→#'   \code{\link{multiblock_biprojector}},
   673→#'   \code{\link{measure_interblock_transfer_error}}
   674→#' @family perm_test
   675→#'
   676→#' @examples
   677→#' # PCA Example
   678→#' data(iris)
   679→#' X_iris <- as.matrix(iris[,1:4])
   680→#' mod_pca <- pca(X_iris, ncomp=4, preproc=center()) # Ensure centering
   681→#'
   682→#' # Test first 3 components sequentially (faster with more nperm)
   683→#' # Ensure a future plan is set for parallel=TRUE, e.g., future::plan("multisession")
   684→#' res_pca <- perm_test(mod_pca, X_iris, nperm=50, comps=3, parallel=FALSE)
   685→#' print(res_pca)
   686→#'
   687→#' # PCA Example with row shuffling (tests different null hypothesis)
   688→#' row_shuffle <- function(dat, ...) dat[sample(nrow(dat)), ]
   689→#' res_pca_row <- perm_test(mod_pca, X_iris, nperm=50, comps=3,
   690→#'                          shuffle_fun=row_shuffle, parallel=FALSE)
   691→#' print(res_pca_row)
   692→#'
   693→#' \dontrun{
   694→#' # Cross Projector Example (using cancor)
   695→#' X <- as.matrix(iris[,1:2])
   696→#' Y <- as.matrix(iris[,3:4])
   697→#' ccr <- cancor(X, Y)
   698→#' mod_cp <- cross_projector(ccr$xcoef, ccr$ycoef)
   699→#'
   700→#' # Perm test (is x2y.mse lower than chance?)
   701→#' res_cp <- perm_test(mod_cp, X, Y=Y, nperm=50, alternative="less")
   702→#' print(res_cp)
   703→#'
   704→#' # Discriminant Projector Example (using LDA)
   705→#' library(MASS)
   706→#' lda_fit <- lda(X_iris, grouping=iris$Species)
   707→#' mod_dp <- discriminant_projector(
   708→#'   v = lda_fit$scaling,
   709→#'   s = X_iris %*% lda_fit$scaling,
   710→#'   sdev = lda_fit$svd,
   711→#'   labels = iris$Species,
   712→#'   preproc = prep(center()), # Assuming center() was intended for LDA
   713→#'   Sigma = lda_fit$covariance # Needed for LDA prediction method
   714→#' )
   715→#'
   716→#' # Perm test (is accuracy higher than chance?)
   717→#' res_dp <- perm_test(mod_dp, X_iris, nperm=50, alternative="greater")
   718→#' print(res_dp)
   719→#'
   720→#' # Multiblock Bi-Projector Example
   721→#' # (Requires a multiblock model 'mod_mb' from e.g. MFA or ComDim)
   722→#' # Assuming 'mod_mb' exists and has 2 blocks:
   723→#' # res_mb <- perm_test(mod_mb, nperm=50, comps=3) 
   724→#' # print(res_mb)
   725→#' # Example using provided Xlist (list of matrices X1, X2):
   726→#' # X1 <- matrix(rnorm(50*10), 50, 10)
   727→#' # X2 <- matrix(rnorm(50*15), 50, 15)
   728→#' # Assume mod_mb was fit on cbind(X1, X2) with block_indices=list(1:10, 11:25)
   729→#' # res_mb_xlist <- perm_test(mod_mb, Xlist=list(X1, X2), nperm=50, comps=3)
   730→#' # print(res_mb_xlist)
   731→#' }
   732→#' @export
   733→perm_test <- function(x, ...) {
   734→  UseMethod("perm_test")
   735→}
   736→
   737→
   738→
   739→#' Screeplot for PCA
   740→#'
   741→#' Displays the variance explained by each principal component as a bar or line plot.
   742→#' 
   743→#' @param x A \code{pca} object.
   744→#' @param ... extra args
   745→#' @export
   746→screeplot <- function(x, ...) UseMethod("screeplot")
   747→
   748→
   749→#' Cross-validation Framework
   750→#'
   751→#' Generic function for performing cross-validation on various objects or data.
   752→#' Specific methods should be implemented for different data types or model types.
   753→#'
   754→#' @param x The object to perform cross-validation on (e.g., data matrix, formula, model object).
   755→#' @param folds A list defining the cross-validation folds, typically containing `train` and `test` indices for each fold.
   756→#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to specific methods.
   757→#'
   758→#' @return The structure of the return value depends on the specific S3 method.
   759→#'   Typically, it will be an object containing the results of the cross-validation,
   760→#'   such as performance metrics per fold or aggregated metrics.
   761→#'
   762→#' @details
   763→#' The specific implementation details, default functions, and relevant arguments vary by method.
   764→#'
   765→#' \strong{Bi-Projector Method (`cv.bi_projector`):}
   766→#' Relevant arguments: \code{x}, \code{folds}, \code{max_comp}, \code{fit_fun},
   767→#'   \code{measure}, \code{measure_fun}, \code{return_models}, \code{...}.
   768→#'
   769→#' This method performs cross-validation specifically for \code{bi_projector} models
   770→#' (or models intended to be used like them, typically from unsupervised methods
   771→#' like PCA or SVD). For each fold, it fits a single model using the training data
   772→#' with the maximum number of components specified (\code{max_comp}). It then iterates
   773→#' from 1 to \code{max_comp} components:
   774→#' \enumerate{
   775→#'   \item It truncates the full model to \code{k} components using \code{truncate()}. 
   776→#'        (Requires a \code{truncate} method for the fitted model class).
   777→#'   \item It reconstructs the held-out test data using the k-component truncated model
   778→#'        via \code{reconstruct_new()}.
   779→#'   \item It calculates reconstruction performance metrics (e.g., MSE, R2) by comparing
   780→#'        the original test data to the reconstruction using the \code{measure} argument
   781→#'        or a custom \code{measure_fun}.
   782→#' }
   783→#' The \code{fit_fun} must accept an argument \code{ncomp}. Additional arguments in \code{...}
   784→#' are passed to \code{fit_fun} and \code{measure_fun}.
   785→#'
   786→#' The return value is a \code{cv_fit} object (a list with class `cv_fit`), where the
   787→#' \code{$results} element is a tibble. Each row corresponds to a fold, containing
   788→#' the fold index (\code{fold}) and a nested tibble (\code{component_metrics}).
   789→#' The \code{component_metrics} tibble has rows for each component evaluated (1 to
   790→#' \code{max_comp}) and columns for the component index (\code{comp}) plus all
   791→#' calculated metrics (e.g., \code{mse}, \code{r2}, \code{mae}) or error messages
   792→#' (\code{comp_error}). If \code{return_models=TRUE}, the full model fitted on the training
   793→#' data for each fold is included in a list column \code{model_full}.
   794→#'
   795→#' @export
   796→#' @family cv
   797→#' @seealso \code{\link{cv_generic}}
   798→cv <- function(x, folds, ...) {
   799→  UseMethod("cv")
   800→}
   801→
   802→
   803→#' Identify Original Variables Used by a Projector
   804→#' 
   805→#' Determines which columns from the *original* input space contribute 
   806→#' (have non-zero influence) to *any* of the output components of the projector.
   807→#' 
   808→#' @param x A projector object (e.g., `projector`, `composed_projector`).
   809→#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to specific methods, such as:
   810→#'   \describe{
   811→#'     \item{`tol`}{Numeric tolerance for determining non-zero coefficients. Default is 1e-8 for some methods.}
   812→#'   }
   813→#' 
   814→#' @return A sorted numeric vector of unique indices corresponding to the original input variables.
   815→#' @export
   816→variables_used <- function(x, ...) {
   817→    UseMethod("variables_used")
   818→}
   819→
   820→#' Identify Original Variables for a Specific Component
   821→#' 
   822→#' Determines which columns from the *original* input space contribute 
   823→#' (have non-zero influence) to a *specific* output component of the projector.
   824→#' 
   825→#' @param x A projector object (e.g., `projector`, `composed_projector`).
   826→#' @param k The index of the output component to query.
   827→#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to specific methods, such as:
   828→#'   \describe{
   829→#'     \item{`tol`}{Numeric tolerance for determining non-zero coefficients. Default is 1e-8 for some methods.}
   830→#'   }
   831→#' 
   832→#' @return A sorted numeric vector of unique indices corresponding to the original input variables.
   833→#' @export
   834→vars_for_component <- function(x, k, ...) {
   835→    UseMethod("vars_for_component")
   836→}
   837→
   838→
   839→
